We’ll keep this short, Team: **The MAGA-ified U.S. Department of Justice has been demanding voter data from the states** , requesting that election officials turn over unredacted copies of voter rolls and every voter registration application submitted in the past 18 months. Several states with MAGA-aligned Secretaries of State and opponents of voting rights in charge of elections have complied, but other states are holding out to protect voters from intimidation, including Maine. **DOJ has escalated their attacks in Maine** , with suppressive former Trump lawyer, now Assistant Attorney General Harmeet Dhillon demanding an electronic copy of the voter registration list that “ _must contain all fields, including the registrant’s full name, date of birth, residential address, his or her state driver’s license number or the last four digits of the registrant’s social security number_ ” from December 1, 2023, through July 1, 2025.Folks, the U.S. Constitution entrusts the states, not the president or his agencies, to run elections. There's no legal basis for the DOJ to demand such sensitive and personally identifying information. **It's yet another overreach by a president fueled by lies and taken by sycophants who are more loyal to his every whim than the rule of law they swore an oath to uphold.** **[Team, voters in Maine and every other state deserve safe, secure, private elections, free of intimidation. >From Murder Capital to Migrant Crisis: Why Trump Must Deploy the National Guard to Chicago (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/08/from-murder-capital-to-migrant-crisis-why-trump-must-deploy-the-national-guard-to-chicago.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=bb509514cc-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/08/from-murder-capital-to-migrant-crisis-why-trump-must-deploy-the-national-guard-to-chicago.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=bb509514cc-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
(John J. Kim/Chicago Tribune)
By Rod Blagojevich and Mark Vargas

While Illinois Gov. J.B. Pritzker and Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson tout drops in homicides, robberies and shootings, the fact is Chicago remains a city crisis.

Need proof?

Chicago continues to lead the nation in murders, shootings and youth victims. In mass shootings, the city would rank second only to California if it were a state.

More than 90 percent of Chicago’s violent offenders roam free each day, with most never even identified or charged.

The city continues to struggle with a severe police shortage, leaving over 1,600 vacancies in the force and fewer officers patrolling the streets.

In 2023 alone, more than 225,000 high-priority 911 calls – such as ‘shots fired’ or ‘assault in progress’ – went unanswered because of the police shortage, putting residents and visitors across the Windy City in grave danger.

After Illinois ended cash bail in September 2023, more than 67,000 defendants skipped their court dates, getting only postcards with new hearings instead of facing arrest.

Since 2022, more than 52,000 migrants have arrived in Chicago, regardless of background or criminal history – costing taxpayers nearly $700 million and putting residents and tourists at serious risk.

In late January, shortly after taking office, President Donald Trump ordered the FBI, ATF, DEA, U.S. Marshals, and Customs and Border Protection to carry out ‘targeted operations’ to arrest dangerous migrants on the streets. The results were clear: convicted sex offenders and child predators were apprehended and removed from communities.

How did these dangerous criminals and predators end up in Chicago? Because Gov. JB Pritzker and Mayor Brandon Johnson support housing illegal immigrants under the city’s sanctuary policies.

On Friday, President Donald Trump commented that he’s thinking about sending the national guard to Chicago to secure the streets after such a deployment proved successful in Washington, DC.

“Chicago is a mess. You have an incompetent mayor, grossly incompetent, and we’ll straighten that one out probably next. That’ll be our next one after this, and it won’t even be tough. And the people in Chicago ... are screaming for us to come ... so I think Chicago will be our next and then we’ll help with New York.”

Pritzker, who maintains presidential ambitions, was quick to comment, accusing Trump of trying to make up a fake crisis.

“Donald Trump is attempting to manufacture a crisis, politicize Americans who serve in uniform, and continue abusing his power to distract from the pain he is causing working families.”

A recent nationwide poll of twelve potential candidates for the 2028 Democratic presidential primary placed Pritzker at the bottom and the least popular, with minority voters giving him the least support as other prominent Democrats prepare for potential campaigns.

In June, President Trump sent the National Guard to Los Angeles under similar conditions, with the Justice Department noting that these deployments don’t constitute law enforcement – they simply assist immigration agents and safeguard federal buildings.

Chicago is at a tipping point. Violent offenders roam free, the police force is stretched beyond its limits and sanctuary policies continue to shield dangerous criminals.

Residents deserve safety, not political posturing. The National Guard has proven effective in other cities – there is no reason to wait while the streets of Chicago remain unsafe.

It’s time for decisive action before more lives are lost.

Rod Blagojevich served as the 40th governor of Illinois.

name: Navigating Uncertainty (by Vikram Mansharamani)
subject: Pepperoni Pizza Politics
body:
The Constitution bans "double jeopardy" for accused criminals; time to offer pepperoni the same protection?

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..ouRSPXOoEQQDUoSoJW5WJM_kMFZxVjAba3YQA3d2YnQ?) for more
---
[](https://substack..JFJbqzgbBkN38j0OEGfDIpmsTv24nbJ_WliiXEXNzf8?)
---
# [Pepperoni Pizza Politics](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=171701825&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNzAxODI1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTYwODM3MDQsImV4cCI6MTc1ODY3NTcwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ol_WCd_mTN_TcjVKPa04S3hkRa5u6yEslEQpnyhIlIU)
### The Constitution bans "double jeopardy" for accused criminals; time to offer pepperoni the same protection?
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---
| Aug 25
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=171701825&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNzAxODI1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NjA4MzcwNCwiZXhwIjoxNzU4Njc1NzA0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTY2MTk5OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.bVVhTSRwfBXE7MFSy58AMayGnwooKLwp6VlElGlB0i4&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=171701825&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNzAxODI1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTYwODM3MDQsImV4cCI6MTc1ODY3NTcwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ol_WCd_mTN_TcjVKPa04S3hkRa5u6yEslEQpnyhIlIU&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=171701825&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNzAxODI1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTYwODM3MDQsImV4cCI6MTc1ODY3NTcwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ol_WCd_mTN_TcjVKPa04S3hkRa5u6yEslEQpnyhIlIU)
---
| | [](https://substack..IYht0ySooF8Wal224V7D4uwmssTDZPTbtF-Ek9AR7_8?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mansharamani/p/pepperoni-pizza-politics?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/49b14dc7-e070-4c43-b1d3-8be8bfbefb1b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
As an academic, author, consultant, entrepreneur, and corporate board member, I have long understood that federal regulations and government overreach were burdening businesses, but as the CEO of a food manufacturing business, I have a specific example worth highlighting.
Consider pepperoni. Goodwell Foods makes private label frozen pizzas for some of the largest retailers in the country and pepperoni pizza is our biggest seller. We will produce millions of pizzas this year and we will use hundreds of thousands of pounds of pepperoni. Because we buy our pepperoni as finished product, it comes to us after it's been inspected and cured. It's ready to eat and has been manufactured in a facility that has a USDA inspector auditing food safety.
As a matter of fact, the very same pepperoni we order is also shipped to supermarkets, pizzerias, and sub shops. Anyone can walk into a grocery store, grab it off the shelf, and eat it as is.
But if they send it to us first, **you can 't**.
Federal regulations mandate the USDA inspect our efforts whenever we handle pepperoni. If we plan to produce pepperoni pizza, we need to notify the USDA in advance so that an official can be on our premises. He checks records, walks the floor, and keeps an eye out for potential issues.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..60msAQsQE1T40Lrb__z7ZWyBHluugVfb9nGPtGvMgCU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=171701825)
Who pays for this inspector? We do. We also must provide a parking space and a place for the inspector to work (we have a dedicated, air-conditioned office for our USDA inspector).
As for the specific USDA official designated to Goodwell, we have been lucky to have been assigned competent professionals who have been easy to work with. In fact, when our first inspector retired from the USDA, we even had a party for him in our breakroom. And I genuinely appreciate the job done by the USDA inspectors. The inspection of slaughterhouses and meat-processing plants, for example, is intensive work, vital to keeping us all healthy. They make sure employees are properly trained, audit cleanliness, and help ensure pathogens remain out of our food. The reason few of us doubt the safety of the meat we buy is because of the good work done by USDA inspectors.
I know inspectors do important work at the plant that makes our pepperoni, and I'm grateful for their efforts. They make sure the pepperoni is cured correctly and handled safely. And it's because of my confidence in their work that I feel they don't need to inspect it again.
It's not so much the cost or the intrusiveness of the whole affair, but rather the illogical, nonsensical rules that nobody can explain to me that bugs me. What is the difference between the pepperoni I put on my pizzas and the pepperoni on the supermarket shelf?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5d84e222-2d66-498a-b7b2-22a002584d86?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
If the pepperoni necessitates re-inspection, shouldn't the USDA have an inspector at every pizzeria? This would of course also mean that you must keep an extra parking spot in your driveway for an inspector when you slice up some pepperoni to add to your charcuterie platter during a dinner party. Alternatively, if the government wants this to all make sense, it could eliminate double-inspection.
Hundreds of years ago, America's Founding Fathers drafted the United States Constitution as the law of the land and a framework for the federal government. As we are aware, the first ten amendments to the Constitution are known as The Bill of Rights and it's worth noting that the Fifth Amendment is about due process. In addition to protecting the accused from testifying against themselves (popularized by "pleading the fifth"), it also explicitly adds that the accused shall not be tried twice for the same crime (known as "double jeopardy").
Let's offer pepperoni the same protections we offer accused criminals. It's time to end the double inspections (and make pepperoni great again).
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..60msAQsQE1T40Lrb__z7ZWyBHluugVfb9nGPtGvMgCU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=171701825)
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=171701825&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNzAxODI1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTYwODM3MDQsImV4cCI6MTc1ODY3NTcwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ol_WCd_mTN_TcjVKPa04S3hkRa5u6yEslEQpnyhIlIU)
[Leave a comment](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=171701825&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNzAxODI1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTYwODM3MDQsImV4cCI6MTc1ODY3NTcwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ol_WCd_mTN_TcjVKPa04S3hkRa5u6yEslEQpnyhIlIU&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c6e2bce1-56e1-4d8a-80e2-7930f0c2a5e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. **He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza**. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/0cae8841-f8b9-4251-ad16-69bb46774cb0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/b5e879ab-a59f-48a9-a90c-d7a2e9c53cfd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/ef8d75f2-c773-4605-8267-a06da806b81c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.

clean_body: The Constitution bans "double jeopardy" for accused criminals; time to offer pepperoni the same protection? | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? The Constitution bans "double jeopardy" for accused criminals; time to offer pepperoni the same protection? | | | Aug 25 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- As an academic, author, consultant, entrepreneur, and corporate board member, I have long understood that federal regulations and government overreach were burdening businesses, but as the CEO of a food manufacturing business, I have a specific example worth highlighting. Consider pepperoni. Goodwell Foods makes private label frozen pizzas for some of the largest retailers in the country and pepperoni pizza is our biggest seller. We will produce millions of pizzas this year and we will use hundreds of thousands of pounds of pepperoni. Because we buy our pepperoni as finished product, it comes to us after it's been inspected and cured. It's ready to eat and has been manufactured in a facility that has a USDA inspector auditing food safety. As a matter of fact, the very same pepperoni we order is also shipped to supermarkets, pizzerias, and sub shops. Anyone can walk into a grocery store, grab it off the shelf, and eat it as is. But if they send it to us first, you can 't. Federal regulations mandate the USDA inspect our efforts whenever we handle pepperoni. If we plan to produce pepperoni pizza, we need to notify the USDA in advance so that an official can be on our premises. He checks records, walks the floor, and keeps an eye out for potential issues. Who pays for this inspector? We do. We also must provide a parking space and a place for the inspector to work (we have a dedicated, air-conditioned office for our USDA inspector). As for the specific USDA official designated to Goodwell, we have been lucky to have been assigned competent professionals who have been easy to work with. In fact, when our first inspector retired from the USDA, we even had a party for him in our breakroom. And I genuinely appreciate the job done by the USDA inspectors. The inspection of slaughterhouses and meat-processing plants, for example, is intensive work, vital to keeping us all healthy. They make sure employees are properly trained, audit cleanliness, and help ensure pathogens remain out of our food. The reason few of us doubt the safety of the meat we buy is because of the good work done by USDA inspectors. I know inspectors do important work at the plant that makes our pepperoni, and I'm grateful for their efforts. They make sure the pepperoni is cured correctly and handled safely. And it's because of my confidence in their work that I feel they don't need to inspect it again. It's not so much the cost or the intrusiveness of the whole affair, but rather the illogical, nonsensical rules that nobody can explain to me that bugs me. What is the difference between the pepperoni I put on my pizzas and the pepperoni on the supermarket shelf? | | ---|---|--- If the pepperoni necessitates re-inspection, shouldn't the USDA have an inspector at every pizzeria? This would of course also mean that you must keep an extra parking spot in your driveway for an inspector when you slice up some pepperoni to add to your charcuterie platter during a dinner party. Alternatively, if the government wants this to all make sense, it could eliminate double-inspection. Hundreds of years ago, America's Founding Fathers drafted the United States Constitution as the law of the land and a framework for the federal government. As we are aware, the first ten amendments to the Constitution are known as The Bill of Rights and it's worth noting that the Fifth Amendment is about due process. In addition to protecting the accused from testifying against themselves (popularized by "pleading the fifth"), it also explicitly adds that the accused shall not be tried twice for the same crime (known as "double jeopardy"). Let's offer pepperoni the same protections we offer accused criminals. It's time to end the double inspections (and make pepperoni great again). | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and . Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Kelan Publishing, LLC PO BOX 932, Lincoln, NH 03251 ? ?

---
Since launching this campaign, I have visited communities all across the Commonwealth — from Farmville to Fairfax, Richmond to Roanoke — to hear directly from Virginians about the challenges they face.
_And that's informed what we communicate and how we spend our resources._
My opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, has shown she's in lockstep with Trump's agenda. She downplayed the White House laying off thousands of Virginia workers by saying, "don't fret." And she boasted that Trump's disastrous tax law does "so many great things." _We're talking about a policy that cuts $1 trillion from Medicaid and slashes resources for public schools here._
I've given voters my word: **As Governor, I will always put them first and stand up to the chaos coming out of Washington.**
We can't be defined by what we're against — we must focus on moving our Commonwealth forward.
That's why I have announced my plans to grow our workforce and raise the minimum wage, to reduce the cost of childcare, and to make life more affordable from healthcare and prescription drugs to housing costs and utility bills.
With the days left in this campaign, I'm counting on a surge of grassroots donations to:
✅ Visit every corner of Virginia.
✅ Reach undecided voters with ads and counter the GOP's attacks.
✅ Build the largest organizing operation our Commonwealth's ever seen.
It's a major undertaking, but I know this team is not intimidated by hard work. No matter what the National Republicans throw at us, we'll be ready to respond.
**[Everything depends on us raising what we need from grassroots donors. So, I'm asking: will you pitch in $10 or more to my campaign and State Democrats Committee to help us win on November 4th and in the years ahead?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94173?refcode=em20250824-9323&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=10&akid=9323%2E614464%2E7C_DNT)**

name: Rob Bonta
subject: Trump is president for now, not forever
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642]
body:
[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/4?refcode=em250824&t=1001&akid=1925%2E1088799%2EWymtL1 ]Rob Bonta

I
was raised by activists.

[ https://act.robbonta.com/survey/em-250824-pet/?t=1002&akid=1925%2E1088799%2EWymtL1 ][IMG]

My parents were two everyday people without fancy titles or large
inheritances to their name, who were inspired to join extraordinary
movements.

My dad marched for civil rights in Selma, Alabama.

Together they stood with farmworkers in the Central Valley.

My mom fought for the restoration of democracy in the Philippines – her
home country and where I was born.

I tagged along to the marches, protests, and community meetings.

And through it all, they taught me that people power is the most potent
power there is.

We the people can take down bullies. Stand up for the downtrodden. Demand
justice, equity, and safety for all people.

And that’s exactly what we need now.

Trump and his allies want to divide us with fear, mistrust, and cruelty.
But I believe — and my parents showed me — that when we stand together, we
are unstoppable.

That means we can’t tune out, no matter how exhausting this fight feels.
We can’t become numb to Trump’s abuse of power.

We must stay engaged. Stay enraged. Protest peacefully. Support Democrats
running for office. Vote — and get your friends and neighbors to vote — in
every single election.

[ https://act.robbonta.com/survey/em-250824-pet/?t=1003&akid=1925%2E1088799%2EWymtL1 ]If you agree, add your name today to show the strength of people
power.

[ https://act.robbonta.com/survey/em-250824-pet/?t=1004&akid=1925%2E1088799%2EWymtL1 ]ADD YOUR NAME

We ought not be hopeless — because we’re not helpless. Trump may be
president for now, but not forever. Together, we’ll make sure of it.

In solidarity,
Rob

[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/1054?t=1005&akid=1925%2E1088799%2EWymtL1 ][IMG]

Rob Bonta
Attorney General of California

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.robbonta.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=1006&akid=1925%2E1088799%2EWymtL1
Update Your Information: https://act.robbonta.com/signup/update-your-information/
Unsubscribe: https://act.robbonta.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1007&akid=1925%2E1088799%2EWymtL1

Rob Bonta for Attorney General 2026
c/o Deane & Company
1700 Tribute Road, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA 95815

name: Aron Johnson
subject: Introducing myself
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2508-q3-jg?refcode=250824-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2508-q3-jg?refcode=250824-email]
Friend —
My name is Aron Johnson, and I’m honored to serve as Jena Griswold’s campaign manager in her race for Colorado Attorney General.
My job is to make sure Jena has the resources, team, and strategy to win — but I can’t do it without you. Your support is the difference between a winning campaign and a campaign that gets drowned out. So, can you chip in right now to power our campaign? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2508-q3-jg?refcode=250824-email]
Jena is exactly the kind of leader Colorado needs — tough, principled, and unafraid to take on the biggest fights. She has stood up to Donald Trump and MAGA extremists, protected Coloradans’ freedoms, and has never backed down when the rights of Coloradans are on the line.
This is my first full month on the campaign, and I have already hit the ground running. We are attending events across the state and doing everything we can to share Jena’s vision for the Attorney General’s office with as many folks as possible.
All of this work helps move the needle - and it all costs money. I want my first month on the campaign to be the strongest one yet - so can you help us elect the Attorney General Colorado needs by chipping in right now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2508-q3-jg?refcode=250824-email]
I’ll be in touch throughout the campaign to share updates and ways you can get involved. Together, we can elect Jena and ensure our state remains a stronghold for democracy.
Thank you,
Aron Johnson
Campaign Manager
Follow Jena Online:
https://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/ [https://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/] https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold [https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold] https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold/ [https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold/] https://www.threads.net/@jenagriswold [https://www.threads.net/@jenagriswold] https://bsky.app/profile/jenagriswold.bsky.social [https://bsky.app/profile/jenagriswold.bsky.social]

clean_body: [] Friend — My name is Aron Johnson, and I’m honored to serve as Jena Griswold’s campaign manager in her race for Colorado Attorney General. My job is to make sure Jena has the resources, team, and strategy to win — but I can’t do it without you. Your support is the difference between a winning campaign and a campaign that gets drowned out. So, can you chip in right now to power our campaign? [] Jena is exactly the kind of leader Colorado needs — tough, principled, and unafraid to take on the biggest fights. She has stood up to Donald Trump and MAGA extremists, protected Coloradans’ freedoms, and has never backed down when the rights of Coloradans are on the line. This is my first full month on the campaign, and I have already hit the ground running. We are attending events across the state and doing everything we can to share Jena’s vision for the Attorney General’s office with as many folks as possible. All of this work helps move the needle - and it all costs money. I want my first month on the campaign to be the strongest one yet - so can you help us elect the Attorney General Colorado needs by chipping in right now? [] I’ll be in touch throughout the campaign to share updates and ways you can get involved. Together, we can elect Jena and ensure our state remains a stronghold for democracy. Thank you, Aron Johnson Campaign Manager Follow Jena Online: [] [] [] [] [] Paid for by Jena for Colorado. Rachel Gordon, Registered Agent. Jena for Colorado P.O. name: Troy Downing
subject: Hey you! (yes, YOU, Peter).
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
Peter, do you consider yourself a true Patriot? If you do, I’m BEGGING YOU to read this email...

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)Hey you!
Yes, you, Peter.
No, not your laptop. The person looking at it!
I was really hoping to reach you today.
This is Congressman Troy Downing, and I want to thank you for opening my message. Can I borrow just one more minute of your time?
**President Donald J. Trump and House Republicans have accomplished a historic eight-month run unlike anything in modern American history.**
_🇺🇸 We secured the border 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We brought back American jobs 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We cut taxes for working Americans 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We reduced government waste 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We eliminated the woke agenda 🇺🇸_ The tide has turned in our favor.
**_BUT!!! BUT!!! BUT!!!_**
**WE CAN’T REST ON OUR LAURELS NOW!!!**
The Democrats are more desperate than ever.
They’ve doubled down on extremism with their unhinged socialist economic policies, woke DEI agenda, and aggressive climate change plan.
They’ve become a dangerous cabal that **CANNOT BE TRUSTED** with America’s future.
If the pro-Trump GOP can hold the majority through 2028 and solidify America’s Golden Age.
But we need the funds to make it happen NOW!
The further we fall behind the Democrats in fundraising, the closer we get to losing our chance.
**_Peter, do you consider yourself a true Patriot? It’s time to prove it._**
I’m not asking you to risk life and limb for your country (though I think you would). I’m just requesting $17 to lock in a Republican House Majority for two more years. [Is it worth it to you?](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-SzXmzvGWA)[CHIP IN $17 RIGHT NOW
](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-3eBJAYC5GA)–Troy| |

body:
**President Trump** has tapped Airbnb co-founder Joe Gebbia as the federal government’s first chief design officer, tasked with improving online government services to be as “satisfying to use as the Apple Store,” according to Gebbia

**Good Morning!** On this day in 1718, [New Orleans was founded](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u4yAQhZ8Gd1gw_iMFxTbusu2WFg4z9qwIRICC_ParrK50H-B2pzjnfNL3577dHQeOx2_3RFspbRFbCVgr5o04l_o4Gclj4SMmIn5gPtIGCiapjIRp847DtY2dt4QIOHRo9TLNWs-DvnWnBTUR7bv2fkA30YKjGXba1XwzA3ijO7afN2Vg0kpN2vSLUTdDeqYBnPJuFKNCzzXl_m_iWE-kcKU35v6Rnl2wZ62vIoZfAlYBa2utj9hSDuhi-VQErPXkeBRZk_RJwHpyqSlf30kmkhGb_FrJ_ZIvzJy8gLXL1r8ah8BlXsSojqfj8B9df-TrbeFfAAAA___M1IC7) by the French under Jean-Baptiste Le Moyne de Bienville. Named for the Duke of Orléans, the city’s French, African, Spanish, and Caribbean influences soon blended into the unique culture that gave rise to jazz, Mardi Gras, and its enduring spirit. Today, we’re reporting on how [America’s favorite baby names](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOqzAQRb_GdEHjMTikcPEaurx2SzTYY5iVwRG2gvL3q-UXtru6xTnS-XpOT5Ik-_KfNnY15mnnsySulY8pylGqX4Vj4CLLnmMUz8eSJwTsbzDcsJ8CSfpMXRNcZEY2DTt9763W1uhHs7rZa4thtoOHTs-GEIz2XcSAYKM1thH3S4MBew3Q66G9D_AYorbRIEGgTnXAQWo-2u8se105pk9-89H6vDXJrbW-ijL_FI4Kx_M821KoXfJb4ZjJV4XjTPNnp42LwjGwp3Ct60HQUNq1bqk5XHidkpIUe1cdLBtJuhz1T2HeDn8CAAD__z-WdmE) have changed from the 1880s to now. What about you—what’s your favorite name for a boy and a girl? Hit reply and let us know. **A quick note regarding our ongoing capital raise:** We’ve hit $1.1 million committed! We have a special video in today’s Own The Flyover section below—don’t miss it. [Watch our CEO, Cole](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0T3r2zAQx_FXI28JerBsZdDQknpLswQ6CkU62Vf0YGQlIe--uJ0C3f7z98cHjvt1MReLEfP80ybQLRST4bVFaA2qCVi35haE4GHDOZcQ0EGdi-GUywNVBy6Ntxjfpu-8DgAcRAeajXJgbBDs1C1aWdc7uDMm72wc4a6CssBG5y0LQ3CnDvWuUcUlo1QydRwVPanAhiC4pd72pKfgsZV6_F0wtwVCfJcn1KMrqYt6aW3diPhG-ET4hPkJW_vPcs9ierRktvKoDog4__DYsGTCB5tWIr7vMYHHRyLifH1l8IcLeLQfA2fTanHORJynf_pnLrlBbjtwmz5Kg7q7t-lqFJdcdlX79YUx4jaMpKdzshj_3tS-9Iin5n8CAAD__1BFnhE), and Founder, Guy, as they pull back the curtain on The Flyover and share our plans for the future. Today's sponsor is [Raisin](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL3O4yAQRZ8Gd474scEpKFaK3GXbLS0Mgz1ZDBbgRHn7lVeR8gBfO0c6c-_9c5_uBgPG5bfZQFefpgivEqBWyJPHXKpdEbyDgktM3qOFvKSJU963dGh5PzmD4T11jdMegINoQDPVS8akYNdm1V5Y5eaZUaaUmaVkVtmrnIVT3jFDWYP6tNGB94zSng0XNdDr4Jn0ghvqTEc6Cg5rypdHwlhX8OGdnpAvNm1N0GuteyHiF-Ej4WM2WDCeiPARYnsUwseyZ4xLO6d4lHZOqVQixqNu0wYOj42I27c14fIkJR3ZAhG3778PsWbbDS6RiNvjiMD7z71CPk1zSPYva7J2-wtDwCIV6eiyGQz_A9cfrfzU_F8AAAD__7qLk7U). ## Trump, Moore Spar Over Baltimore Crime
President Trump has offered to [deploy the National Guard to Baltimore](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO8yAQBOCnwZ0jWIwhBcXfuMvfXmkRs9h7whDhvUS5pz_5XuHqkebTzMdtvgXKVNb_YUfPqc4FX0dGZmxzonbwshGmiAetpaZEC7a1ziDB9NL1YOYYKL_noYs-IQLqDr2yZlRq1OrabT4FfXdxdBZABQdgpNXWao3B4H24jx35s006MEpKo9zFOnl1SY1JQ5AxDGKQGIlru3xWKrxhyu_6xHZZ6t5lvzE_DqH_CZgETLzhRjmfmYBpqzuegwRMIe5U6OAWmGoRMJlhtFcpe25f-6NfGjEt9I1Hv4f2zqHEfj2VUpuAqWs-Pl6UMx2jFYNc90C_SMd_eu3p4ScAAP__rXmBqQ) in response to Maryland Gov. Wes Moore’s (D) social media post telling the president to “come walk the streets with us.” The online feud centers on the president deploying troops to Washington, D.C., to curb crime there and suggesting last week that he may do the same in other Democratic-led cities with [high crime rates](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkE9rhDAUxD9NvCnJyx_1kEOhCD1s6a1HyZoXfSUaidld9tsXS0-99jjzg2FmPi_jxVGkbX53K9oS0rjh44hYCuYxUD7KtBAGjwfNWwqBJsxzGoGDrnlXgx69o_gcVeVtQASUFVrRaiOEkaKvFuu4axV6qbEVLTdm0i704QqyR-3NhBXZM413oAXnWnRN2_G-C8IECY57p5ji6Kmk3Hwl2sqCIT7THXMzpbWKdillP5h8YTAwGNx1Ohc0czoxg-EjRSo0HQyGSHesb7t3BU9Z8m3da-dX2uo_iMmBPJOvAlQrVa84A4Nbyc-3X7eXUvRQZev3B8VIh2mZ4vPqKP7UKv_68m7hOwAA__81poX_), including Baltimore. In the exchange, President Trump questioned whether Moore had ever received a Bronze Star for military service and said he might “[rethink](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHSnSAUBOCn0Y47gOjFgiKN3Z82pYNwkJMgOHDu7_j2GfMKqb-Znd399bV-WUyY95_2AEOhrBmuloAI6hqwNnIRIXhouOcSAjqoe1kllyPjmslx9RbTvaremwAgYejBiPc4CTENYu6jsWLmdt605MrBW3nON7UFP8M46Flz26N50riWo-B8FPr11nzWQUxhkJZ7qzrFwSOV-vpdMFOEkO7yDfXlytEnE4nO1g0_Orl0crmu65U396x4vJPLWRISuvZgRAIWy6dBJxeqn-NkFCtYgtzYgQnJ1pttNhEepQJzFQ9gf-BmW0W_Awuf7DHvzEV0di-sumylnLSa-mr8eWFK2KZ3p_h-WEz_KtJ__fpt5N8AAAD__wgPj1Y)” funding repairs to the Francis Scott Key Bridge, which collapsed in 2024. _“After only one week, there is NO CRIME AND NO MURDER IN DC! When it is like that in Baltimore, I will proudly ‘walk the streets’ with the failing... Governor of Maryland,”_ Trump wrote.
## Airbnb Co-Founder Named First US Design Chief
President Trump named Airbnb co-founder Joe Gebbia as the [U.S.' first chief design officer](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO4yAURb_G7ogAG4MLipVW7rLtlhaGh3kjDBaQRPn7kTOfMOV7V7rn6vy_r3eDEdP-zxygm89rgleN0BqU1WOpzQYE76DinrL3aKHseeWUC0IV4WJ1BuN7HXunPQCHoQfNpJgYmwY290Fba_mk7GZnP0vuJmDCCkGFN8qPUrEe9dVGFReMUsHUTSo6K88mP3BDnRm7kYLDlsvtK2NqAXx85yeUm81HH3Vo7azd8KfjS8eXFiBgjFfW8eXMEe37eoMNKce8X4cYJymHkbTyOE5SIYJtlRgsW9qIzcTnR3JQiKnko4DsFy4dkBr5MUECGHcBh6VtBXw3_A1nX7Q7Xxgj1kl2I90Pg58hffuV2afm3wEAAP__PEuOcw), tasking him with modernizing federal services. Gebbia will head the newly created National Design Studio, aiming to make government websites and tools [as user-friendly as the Apple Store](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOhCAQBuCnwQ4DKMoWFNfY7bVXGhZmdC4gBrk1-_YX9x7hukkm-f98_9d9vjuKtC2fLoGtmOcNziNCrVBmpHJUvxJggIOWLSOSh7LkWQmluTBc6Tk4iq-5b4JFAAVdA1aOepBy6OStWS2OD3wIP47GCbx5j9pJr4N3HfauewwN2StNGKWlEFqadjTiZlAO2CkngutZLyBQzaX9zrTVFTC-8hNK63Nqol1r3Q_WfTA1MTWd59m-3Jrz9WVqujhMTa5U8hGus5aftPM_EX_ruKN0cEp7yU_gSupBGt3Ldq0pNsWG_aQY6RhG1oslOYrv5vqvuZ5W_QYAAP__YTR_rQ). The role falls under the America by Design initiative, with agencies expected to show improvements by July 4, 2026. The studio will shut down after three years.
## America’s Top Names from 1880s to Today
New visualizations displaying the [top 20 most-used names](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOqzAQRb_GdEHjMTikcPEaurx2SzTYY5iVwRG2gvL3q-UXtru6xTnS-XpOT5Ik-_KfNnY15mnnsySulY8pylGqX4Vj4CLLnmMUz8eSJwTsbzDcsJ8CSfpMXRNcZEY2DTt9763W1uhHs7rZa4thtoOHTs-GEIz2XcSAYKM1thH3S4MBew3Q66G9D_AYorbRIEGgTnXAQWo-2u8se105pk9-89H6vDXJrbW-ijL_FI4Kx_M821KoXfJb4ZjJV4XjTPNnp42LwjGwp3Ct60HQUNq1bqk5XHidkpIUe1cdLBtJuhz1T2HeDn8CAAD__z-WdmE) by decade show how America’s naming preferences have changed from the 1880s to today. [For girls](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLtuhDAQAL8GOiPbvEzhIg3dpU2J9uw1bGQwsjeH-Pvo8gvpR6PRfD2WB1CkY_2EHS2HtBx4lYjMmJdAubDbCIPHQuuRQiCHeU2LlroX0gjdLx4o3ktXexsQNbY1WjX2g1JDq6Z6s0oHGZ697npphuCdmrQbuzBKPY1SOajJvm3S6F5J2SvTjEZOJqghtBqkh67qJHrilJvvRAdvGOKdXpgbl_Y62o35LFX7Uem50vN1Xc2Lyg9EBycxRCr8Bis9w0E7MHrhNsgsYMdMDorgdIoDdiwipCxWyrGI5y08OvAolDFScPJwV3qus_XnRTFSGcaqk-sOFP86-F_zXlb_BgAA__-zV4R4), Mary holds the top spot from the 1880s to the 1950s, but parents now prefer less traditional names for their daughters. None of the top 20 girl names from the 1880s appear in the top 20 for the 2010s. [Boys’ names](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDuOpTAQAE8DmZFtPobAwSZkb9MNUWO3oVcGI7vnIW4_enOFyUulUv17LS-gSOf2Fw60HNJy4l0iMmNeAuXCbicMHgttZwqBHOYtLVrqXshR6H7xQPFZutrbgKixrdEq0w9KDa2a6t2awazGe9cOq4FJSukDBgPS6EH1fppqsh-bHHWvpOzV2JhRTmNQQ2g1SA9d1Un0xCk3_xOdvGOIT3pjblw66mh35qtU7Z9Kz5We7_tu3lS-IDq4iCFS4Q9Y6RlOOoDRC7dDZgEHZnJQBKdLnHBgESFlsaaniPURHh14FGocpeDk4an0XGfrr5tipDKYqpPbARR_MvhX795WfwcAAP__qdyEhg), on the other hand, have held more steady, with William and James holding perennial spots in the top 20. Biblical names like Noah, Jacob, and Elijah are among the most-used names for boys now. Analysis of the most recent data shows that Noah is the most popular boy's name and Emma is the most popular girl's name.

On this day in 1718, by the French under Jean-Baptiste Le Moyne de Bienville. Named for the Duke of Orléans, the city’s French, African, Spanish, and Caribbean influences soon blended into the unique culture that gave rise to jazz, Mardi Gras, and its enduring spirit. Today, we’re reporting on how have changed from the 1880s to now. What about you—what’s your favorite name for a boy and a girl? Hit reply and let us know. A quick note regarding our ongoing capital raise: We’ve hit $1.1 million committed! We have a special video in today’s Own The Flyover section below—don’t miss it. , and Founder, Guy, as they pull back the curtain on The Flyover and share our plans for the future. Today's sponsor is . Unlock their access to high-yield savings accounts and make your money work for you. | Trump, Moore Spar Over Baltimore Crime President Trump has offered to in response to Maryland Gov. Wes Moore’s (D) social media post telling the president to “come walk the streets with us.” The online feud centers on the president deploying troops to Washington, D.C., to curb crime there and suggesting last week that he may do the same in other Democratic-led cities with , including Baltimore. In the exchange, President Trump questioned whether Moore had ever received a Bronze Star for military service and said he might “” funding repairs to the Francis Scott Key Bridge, which collapsed in 2024. “After only one week, there is NO CRIME AND NO MURDER IN DC! When it is like that in Baltimore, I will proudly ‘walk the streets’ with the failing... Governor of Maryland,” Trump wrote. Airbnb Co-Founder Named First US Design Chief President Trump named Airbnb co-founder Joe Gebbia as the , tasking him with modernizing federal services. Gebbia will head the newly created National Design Studio, aiming to make government websites and tools . The role falls under the America by Design initiative, with agencies expected to show improvements by July 4, 2026. The studio will shut down after three years. America’s Top Names from 1880s to Today New visualizations displaying the by decade show how America’s naming preferences have changed from the 1880s to today. , Mary holds the top spot from the 1880s to the 1950s, but parents now prefer less traditional names for their daughters. None of the top 20 girl names from the 1880s appear in the top 20 for the 2010s. , on the other hand, have held more steady, with William and James holding perennial spots in the top 20. Biblical names like Noah, Jacob, and Elijah are among the most-used names for boys now. Analysis of the most recent data shows that Noah is the most popular boy's name and Emma is the most popular girl's name. subject: Hotter'N Hell Hundred, 1,000-Word Essays, and a Blue Bell Recall
body:
Thousands of cyclists participated in the 44th annual Hotter’N Hell Hundred bike race in Wichita Falls on Saturday. The temperature hit 94 degrees by 3 p.m., but most riders had crossed the finish line before then.

## Lawsuit Challenges New Districts
A group known as the "Gonzales Plaintiffs" filed a lawsuit [challenging the legality](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2yqyAUhZ8GOx0gKFJQ3MYutz2ls5VtZGYLGSDBvP0Z8wqnWs36mfX93Oc7ePLh8R8OtOXc4hywZsJSMM17vCTsSLS_gkvoBOe8xjRLLvuWj63sZweePrNqnF24M6JBK3Q_CGEGY5rdaq1XszlQGoweUaxjz4dFa2VG5LD0jbdXGR9lLwQXN9ktCOqmYBGbMU5vK1McnS8xdWXHgifkjT7xjalb49GQ3Ut5Znb7x-TE5FRr7bZ4anjl4sNlYXK6PjE5fcPtGsMjYc4-BqD2gGduCWp--dIk657VE_k8aKb44wBP35XyNzZvK38DAAD__wJbea8) of the Republican-backed congressional redistricting plan hours after it passed the Senate early Saturday morning. The lawsuit is an amendment to an ongoing challenge to the map passed following the 2020 census. It [alleges](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGSpSAURL8GMyzgiUJAsInZbLqhhXKVu3WVKcBh3t9vOb-wUWenT_efj-XDI-F1_PYnuPq9p-WCVghqhbzE9MQVgSjeV8gQpBCipbwooTQXhiu9BI_0XoYuuFUEKztwctKjlHa0tovOjKOW2kx6lcbKzYRBGa-0XIdJb7sdO3QPTBilpRTypfoV_PAa_Cp3a8O0b2wQELCm3NcIFb592emdviD3Wzo7crHWz8Jev5iamZpba_3fO2OpfcoHU_Ozh6n5KWFqFoap-QfCY7oL8BWJeMi-FY7nCQF9BU6-lRsr90Rw4HXw7Df0RG8O14EXQIbAH4Xob2Jq7rILnw2JsIwTG8RxeqQfvfp_p3459S8AAP__e4uMEA) that the new plan creates malapportioned districts and was drawn with the intent to dilute the voting power of minorities. Meanwhile, State Rep. Briscoe Cain (R-Deer Park) is the [first candidate](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2SpCAUhZ9GMyxA1CYg2MRsNt3QusJVqELogtvD9Ntv2a8w0UlOnZ_v39f2BSGGdP6FCw39HHlL2GpEIiybz7ckjzH6V3IFneCct1w2yeXE-IPJaXMQ4ntTvTM7d1r0aMQyzULoWevem0XudpEaFSxcP8QsRjU5JyZ-WCWFXfpg7jD-kJMQXIxy2BHUqGAXh9ZuOWynOLpAuQzkkfAH6hHf-RvLYPPVR-OJnrUb_3Ry7eTaWhvuC0fJF3msBIT1dnZyhULBRuzk6rEgg4Ks4PO1x2AhVdZ8Zlc4PbHySuzTxBK2-GauQEvM5nQWrDXkBJFVBKp9Me7ZQoyhzkun-HlBiJ9d9Dua30b-DwAA___Yf4yg) to announce he is running for one of five new Republican-leaning districts. The 9th Congressional District, currently held by U.S. Rep. Al Green (D-Houston), backed Kamala Harris by 44% last year. It was redrawn to include voters who supported President Trump by 20%.
## Fort Hood Tests Expanded Dining Pilot Program
Over the past two weeks, 200 Fort Hood soldiers have participated in a U.S. Army pilot program that [provides expanded dining options](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDvOpDAMx08TuqDEPFOk2Ibu23ZLlCEGLAWMksww3H6VvcJWtmTr93_8-Zl_HAU6t9_uQJu_K88n3ilgzhjnncs4dwxhf58-otdKqZvjDAo6qUYJ3ewdhWduK29fyhtdodVD12ttemOq3Zqx6UboV4dGYwteLaZtYBlVox30g6nIFpgaodNa6QbqF7q2ad1Lr8b4YV1Eq9BT5ljnHTN-XVrDwx-M9cJHFeye85VE80vAJGC677t28XgyHZjKh4CpRBIwPfyOstwETEVTwKTGsoOA6aLAWV6Rt-gO6bJcOWa5M3u50QeTTBw8YUzy4IjS00nnJvnKxGeBV9H666YQKPWDaNV2OAr_HOb_6_Vj4W8AAAD__6jRi_c) for those who use their Common Access Card to purchase meals. The Flexible Eating and Expanded Dining (FEED) program allows soldiers to [use their meal entitlements](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D3u3CAQBfDT4A6LwfiDgiKNu02b0sKesUHBZgXssnv7yLnCv5qRnvR70vvzWB7WB38dv-1Jpnz2uFxUc6BSKC0u3udyFIJ7XZgIQQhRY1qkkD0XE5f9gtaH76IaNKtADQ0ZGPsBQA9aN84M2GulJrLdhLgJCWrdRoBx2xTYsZeNNzcmJtkDCOhku5JVnbIr7FrjuG9MCUJfYmqLo0Ifm_fwjW9K7RbPJhhXyjOz7heTM5NzrbX9W8vnDpmcb5vJWUz3D0zONp1f_sr-OvgeU-EuRuQ5BvSUMi-UC7-o8p0I-TPFI9mbaZLBZ_Uh-DyMTInjtD787y8_W-1t5L8AAAD__-YYf7k) at on-base restaurants like Subway, Starbucks, Qdoba, and Burger King, which are open later than the dining facility. Military dietitians have selected items available for purchase through the program. In addition to providing flexibility, the program aims to [collect data on soldiers' preferences](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOhCAURb8GOw3vgagFxTZ2s-2WBuUxkKBMgB1n_n7jJvsFW93k3uTm5HzdlpsJMRz3T7OTri-XloPOEqlWyotPVxyeYvTfh81kgXN-prwgx77lY4v9Yk2I70U2Vq_cTtCQhqFXAJOapsZrWs24CYUSxLYpNygU_UDUrwSjkMiboK8zPmIPwEFgt5KRQpoV3DTZwW1McrKhptxVT5Veprj4Tk_K3Zb2Jmpf66Mw8cFwZjif59mVmsODyrUznNdsjs1TYTibvL8Zzn_0IBnOLuXa-pRsW1K0gXJpdzKxtO7qYByUHBV0vu6xydo-zhBjKGpgkt93E-IvRf2fu6fGnwAAAP__1-6BAQ). "_It's about accessibility. If a soldier can't make it to a dining facility, that soldier now has somewhere to go to get a meal without forfeiting their entitlements_ ," [logistics specialist Rick Bennett said](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuOhSAYhJ9GOww3FQqKbezOtlsakJ8DCYqBf8_l7TeeV9hqipnMZL6f23qzKafj_m13MPgKZT3g2TIgQl1jueSIkHP8PXwFzyilz1JXTvlIqCJ8XL1N-b3K3htHvWY9GDaPE2N60rqPZgrSBc00szNnnju7OWEnG7TjauaC9clcZVTxkTHKBB8cWCmkdSxo7eewdZKCT1jqgBEQXraF_C4PqMNW9j6biHi2Tnx1fOn4EkrFWIpvcGA6IF-hji_Xq44vtu5vgtCwkQDgyZlyQXLWcq92__iYtgyrdNukYAPi_TgT6elIlABBRlBuEsopqfUQcc99Nf58ppxTm-ZO0vtu02ezx__hfBj-FwAA__8J_ojQ).
## Thousands Compete in Hotter'N Hell Hundred
Thousands of cyclists participated in the [44th annual Hotter’N Hell Hundred](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2O6yAQBeCnwR0W4F8Kitu4y223tAYYAhI2EbAhefuVV3mDraaZGZ3vfN32G4QYzvt_OFDVl0v7ia1ErBXz7tM1To8x-u_TZrScMdZS3gUTE2UrFdNuIcT3PnZWaWYl71DxZZo5l7OUnVfzYJcRVrZYnOXs9KC11cLgygc5rQ66oK5nbBUT54wPotcI4zCC5k5KuzhDRoY21JT76rHiC4qL7_TE3Jt0dFH5Wh-FDP-I2IjYWmt9xVc6oPh04APueO0RsV0wIjbzNjGUWqjO8ERaPdKPk15Q-pHSDAZpOGkLxocK1EGM132XlX20EGMo80JGdj8gxN8o9W8FPpX4CQAA__9JVIdy) bike race in Wichita Falls on Saturday. The temperature [hit 94 degrees](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykULuO4zAQ-xq5i6GXY6tQccDBXa690pA942gA2QqkSZz8_cKL_YOtCBIESfD_bboFSrTf_4UNPb_XPO141ITMWKaYT9gjphSfOxQEJaU8cpm01N1FDhfdTRAofSbbgJ8lONWgV313VcpdnWuit52aLWjt3GAH43orUa-udz2g62a1NOTPMDnoTimpjG5nDNbYMKvVOejXRViJQJxLyxEZ36Gu6ZNfWNolb03ykflRhfkj9Cj0eBxHy7Rh2AEC4-k5VQwcsQg9Pms4OS2ROFzWkFIVeoxUORdahBkjCPP3Z5JpiofHQSlRvfbCyvsWKH338u_eenn9FQAA___3KHyK) by 3 p.m., but most riders had [crossed the finish line](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykUL2upSAQfhrsMDCCSEGxDd3ddkuDMB7IRTGI13PefuO-wlYzyffl-_vzNX-5lNP--u02NO29lnnH-8zYGtY5lufsEXOO1x4qBs4Yu0udgYGkbKIg5-BS_syiC2ZhQfMODVdy5FyPWnfRjF4KKQOX0nmFGrziSocxjHJchViwS-YRYxNIzhkfoF_QiUG4ha9aB7V6IhiG1ErtW8SGb3eu-VN-sPa-bF02sbXjJMMvApaAve-7b2nDs6IvNTxtHh4BeyTfror05XLG-iFgH5CAffwJWDY9vyBgfUT_TcvV6BFLKydda9loi0hjjBT3cFW3e6RL-kZaU0ACdpJKDFxrxhQB21UTjjvlnM5REcFem0v5X972fyv_GPgbAAD__5Abjy4) before then. With routes between 10 kilometers and 100 miles, _USA Today_ rates the race the [No. 3 road cycling event](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OgzAQhJ_GdCDv8uvCxTV0ufZKtLBLsOTgyHZCePsT9wpXTTGjGc33c5tu5Lzb79_0EJs_a5h2OZKXnCVOW7hk38T77bVzFAat9RHihBrbUg8lthOT8-fUFGxnzQYKsdC3HYDpjCk2O_QzQ90NQrQgaoRW08y4SL9i04ApnL3K9IAtgIYaq1moqRuaYTWG-3VRjRZ2OcQqb5LlQ2n1Z3hLrJbwKLzdcn4mVX8pHBWOoGdJuXolyoHpvDIKRzooclI4XmYZA3G5nMt1vJS37FnhWETLz8N571LXq0bfH-T830T-H5i3xd8AAAD__-vzdxc) in the country. clean_body: Thousands of cyclists participated in the 44th annual Hotter’N Hell Hundred bike race in Wichita Falls on Saturday. The temperature hit 94 degrees by 3 p.m., but most riders had crossed the finish line before then. | | | | | | Monday, August 25, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1917, the of the U.S. Army was activated at Camp Travis in San Antonio. Composed of members from Texas and Oklahoma, the division became known as the "Tough 'Ombres." Blue Bell has recalled one of its popular flavors because of improper packaging. Check out in today's Et Cetera section. Just a quick note regarding our ongoing capital raise: We’ve hit $1.1 million committed! We have a special video in today’s Own The Flyover section below—don’t miss it. Watch our CEO, Cole, and Founder, Guy, as they pull back the curtain on The Flyover and share our plans for the future. . Today's sponsor is . Check out their weight loss and nutrition tips in today's edition. Abilene ☁️ 97/71° | Amarillo ☁️ 87/68° | Austin 🌤️ 98/76° | Brownsville ☁️ 93/76° | Corpus Christi ⛈️ 87/78° | DFW ☁️ 99/76° | El Paso ☁️ 97/70° | Houston 🌤️ 95/74° | Laredo ☁️ 95/76° | Lubbock ☁️ 95/70° | Midland 🌥️ 96/72° | San Antonio ☁️ 99/74° | Tyler ☁️ 96/72° | Waco ☁️ 98/74° Take The Flyoverwith you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | to hear directly from Cole and Guy. | Lawsuit Challenges New Districts A group known as the "Gonzales Plaintiffs" filed a lawsuit of the Republican-backed congressional redistricting plan hours after it passed the Senate early Saturday morning. The lawsuit is an amendment to an ongoing challenge to the map passed following the 2020 census. It that the new plan creates malapportioned districts and was drawn with the intent to dilute the voting power of minorities. Meanwhile, State Rep. Briscoe Cain (R-Deer Park) is the to announce he is running for one of five new Republican-leaning districts. The 9th Congressional District, currently held by U.S. Rep. Al Green (D-Houston), backed Kamala Harris by 44% last year. It was redrawn to include voters who supported President Trump by 20%. Fort Hood Tests Expanded Dining Pilot Program Over the past two weeks, 200 Fort Hood soldiers have participated in a U.S. Army pilot program that for those who use their Common Access Card to purchase meals. The Flexible Eating and Expanded Dining (FEED) program allows soldiers to at on-base restaurants like Subway, Starbucks, Qdoba, and Burger King, which are open later than the dining facility. Military dietitians have selected items available for purchase through the program. In addition to providing flexibility, the program aims to . "It's about accessibility. If a soldier can't make it to a dining facility, that soldier now has somewhere to go to get a meal without forfeiting their entitlements ," . Thousands Compete in Hotter'N Hell Hundred Thousands of cyclists participated in the bike race in Wichita Falls on Saturday. The temperature by 3 p.m., but most riders had before then. With routes between 10 kilometers and 100 miles, USA Today rates the race the in the country. It was to celebrate Wichita Falls' 100th anniversary, and it for the local economy every year. Midwestern State University sophomore Gabbrielle Wrightsman of the 100-mile race in a time of 3:49:09.8, and Gabriel Porterfield in 3:47:19.7. "I beat my opponent to the line, and she’s crazy fast, so I was really excited about it ," said Wrightsman. | Flying together with our sponsor You must have already heard by now about the Ozempic craze... The miracle drug used to treat type II diabetes and hoarded by celebrities and TikTok influencers who want to lose weight in a short period of time. If you’ve been considering trying it, know that it comes with horrifying side effects, like extreme nausea or sagging and premature aging of your skin. Fortunately, a rogue weight loss doctor has invented this . It is based only on plants and it comes at a much cheaper price! You can use it from the comfort of your home, without any needles and with no side effects. And the results? Faster, more efficient and way safer. Find out all about it here: | ➤ A Friday Texas House committee hearing on a bill that would require people in public buildings to use the bathroom corresponding to their biological sex grew heated at times during hours of testimony. One lawmaker reprimanded a witness for making inflammatory comments. () ➤ Meanwhile , Texas House leadership has issued letters to more than 50 quorum-breaking Democrats demanding payment of nearly $10,000 in fines each. The amount includes $500 for each day of the quorum break and the cost of law enforcement efforts to locate the lawmakers. () ➤ Dallas: Police officers have been authorized to wear cowboy hats while on duty. In a Facebook post featuring the Love Field Airport Unit, the department said it is "proudly bringing Texas style to Dallas skies." () ➤ Fort Worth: Former Dallas police chief Eddie Garcia has been hired as the city's next chief of police. "We will defend our community, and all criminals will have every reason to fear this police department ," he said at a news conference. () ➤ Williamson County: The families of five women who died in a wrong-way crash in Burnet County last month have filed multiple lawsuits against the driver of the truck that collided with their vehicle. Kody Lane Talley has been charged with manslaughter and is in jail on a $1 million bond. () ➤ Houston: Protesters who briefly blocked traffic during the CERAWeek oil and gas convention earlier this year were required to write 1,000-word essays on peaceful protests as part of an agreement to dismiss the charges against them. () ➤ Austin: The police department has acquired two electric patrol cars as part of a pilot project. The vehicles are manufactured by General Motors, can run for up to 16 hours at a time, and can accelerate from zero to 60 miles per hour in four seconds. name: Paul Dans, Candidate for U.S. Senate
subject: I created Project 2025, now I am running to unseat this RINO
body:
Help me FIRE Lindsey Graham.

| | | | [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b1Ui/?__vbtrk=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
---
Fellow patriot, I need your support to help retire Lindsey Graham.
I know that South Carolina deserves a conservative champion and if we are going to deliver on the America First agenda that gave Republicans control of the Senate then we cannot afford to send a swamp creature like Graham back to Congress.
**[I’m Paul Dans, founder of Project 2025, and I’m running to knock Lindsey Graham out of the Senate in the 2026 Republican primary.](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b1Ui/?__vbtrk=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**
Graham has spent the past**** 30 years in Washington**,** cozying up to the Uniparty and**** playing both sides**,** while our conservative values get tossed aside.
The facts don’t lie:

* **He’s the king of flip-flopping.** The people of South Carolina know this. They know that Graham is a RINO whose values shift whichever way the wind blows, and it’s why he has a dismal** 34%** approval rating from voters.
* **He’s one of the biggest allies of the Uniparty.** Graham’s record as Senator shows that time and again he has delivered for the D.C. establishment that wants to increase the size of government but not the people voting for him that voted for their Senator to put America First.
* **He’s one of the fiercest advocates for involving our nation in endless wars.** Lindsey pushes for a war-hawk doctrine that has failed America. While our nation suffers at home he has been egging Trump on to bomb Iran, pushed for us to join endless foreign conflicts, and he even got slapped with an international arrest warrant for supporting Ukraine too fiercely.
* **And he’s out of touch with Americans who suffer because of his failed leadership.** Which is why South Carolina conservatives are booing him at Trump rallies. They too know just how detached from the real issues that matter to folks like you.

His rack record is abysmal. We can do better for South Carolina, and we can do better for America.
**[Lindsey Graham is too entrenched in the Swamp, he is too compromised by special interests, and it’s time for a change.](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b1Ui/?__vbtrk=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**
Right now, Republicans have control of the White House, the Senate, and the House. Additionally, our party controls state houses across the country and the majority of the Supreme Court are constitutional conservatives.
**This is a once in a generation moment to make consequential change for our country. That’s what Americans voted for.**
If you feel that the mandate given to conservative Republicans last fall isn’t being delivered it’s because of politicians like Lindsey Graham who have no interest in change. We cannot waste this moment and that’s why I am running to send him packing.

[FIRE LINDSEY GRAHAM](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b1Ui/?__vbtrk=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Friend, at Project 2025,**I helped design a blueprint to shrink government, secure our borders, protect family values, and to put America First.**
Now I’m bringing that battle to the Senate to forge a new path for South Carolina and America**.**
But let's be real: Graham’s massive war chest and insider network isn’t going to go down without a fight.
You and I both know Graham is going to go out on a crusade against me, he will launch massive smear campaigns, tarnish my good name, and lie to deceive voters.
That’s why I am reaching out to conservatives across the country to stand with me as I launch my campaign.
**It’s never been more crucial for me to build up my grassroots army so I can launch our hard-hitting programs to drown out their attacks and expose the TRUTH to the people of South Carolina.**
But without the support of patriots like you, I'll be fighting with one hand tied behind my back against Lindsey’s war chest.
**_[I need your help - and I need it today](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b1Ui/?__vbtrk=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_****.**

[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b1Ui/?__vbtrk=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
[JOIN TEAM DANS](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b1Ui/?__vbtrk=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
This is our chance to take down one of Washington’s biggest villains that have been dragging our country through the mud.**__**
**__**
**_Fights like these are the only way we can ensure our party is filled with conservatives who actually work to put America First so we can take our country back once and for all._**

Remember in 2024, South Carolina proudly stood with Trump and his agenda.Now, let's give him a Senator who believes in the principles of MAGA and will deliver that agenda for the American people -******not betray us.****__**
**__**
**_[Join our fight today by chipping in $10, $25, or whatever you can afford to help me retire Lindsey Graham](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b1Ui/?__vbtrk=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_****.**
Together we’ll overcome Lindsey Graham and his donors and send a message to the Senate that we will fire any politician that fails to deliver and abuses their power.

_For America and South Carolina,_

**Paul Dans**
Candidate for U.S. Senate, South Carolina
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b1Ui/?__vbtrk=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzEzMzUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

subject: 72 days until GOP checkmate blow that could end it all
body:
Say goodbye to Dems ever holding Congress again.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001BwaoWunSo4olDPPebWJz_Ewm3AN77g67vnZOqXo7IIaQCqz90R2IeGDfUVpyZq6F0VsHhYmgJ0-Ckgt3ZcneYIUkB-A6J-UQaLk8MLRp7gasyi5Eroh8zb89-Kv4HOwRHxvtHhsirOCLWzeVDa35KPqrCldUosXzv0Duq7y1Nq9haS9rWkthlKpqsHuDsCnVNbI9w44ra3r4B-fahO7EKs1rFmxTtRYV&c=fJQnG0_lnIrAE9XIMdlvA3NLWe8GF3v1Y4Ugq8eMavfIMyXvMNaLAw==&ch=1vL4i9ToKLjaMZdoDM7PuiEXyXlsUcXUB9av-ianL5uk1c5b0EPNlg==)
---
| Peter,
In 72 days, the GOP are on track to seize the Pennsylvania State Supreme Court in an obscure judicial election.
The consequences of failing to act here are immense.
---
| [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001BwaoWunSo4olDPPebWJz_Ewm3AN77g67vnZOqXo7IIaQCqz90R2IeGDfUVpyZq6F0VsHhYmgJ0-Ckgt3ZcneYIUkB-A6J-UQaLk8MLRp7gasyi5Eroh8zb89-Kv4HOwRHxvtHhsirOCLWzeVDa35KPqrCldUosXzv0Duq7y1Nq9haS9rWkthlKpqsHuDsCnVNbI9w44ra3r4B-fahO7EKkeLrWPMtqXs&c=fJQnG0_lnIrAE9XIMdlvA3NLWe8GF3v1Y4Ugq8eMavfIMyXvMNaLAw==&ch=1vL4i9ToKLjaMZdoDM7PuiEXyXlsUcXUB9av-ianL5uk1c5b0EPNlg==)
---
| | | | Even though states like California are stepping up to retaliate to Trump's redistricting push, Republicans hold the advantage, controlling state legislatures and governorships of 23 states, compared with 15 for Democrats.
So if the gerrymandering war continues to play out and every Red state and every Blue states draws new hyperpartisan maps, Democrats lose.
This is why the newest front in this fight is coming down to swing state supreme courts. MAGA tried earlier this year to take the Supreme Court in Wisconsin but was repelled and now they are trying to do the same thing in PA this November.
Republican capture of the court in PA would be a checkmate blow for democrats, a coup de grace. We rely on PA for seven house seats and are on track to pick up two to four red PA house seats if trends continue and Democrats sweep the upcoming midterms.
Losing this court means that Pennsylvania's vulnerable red seats will be shored up and all of our blue seats will be erased just like they were in Texas. Say goodbye to Dems ever holding Congress again.
[We have 72 days to prevent this, Republicans are on the march and time is running out for Democrats to make a comeback. Defend our Courts is organizing the defense of the democratic justices on the Pennsylvania Supreme Court being targeted for removal. Can we count on you to make a donation of whatever you can spare so we can immediately invest it into saving this race?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001BwaoWunSo4olDPPebWJz_Ewm3AN77g67vnZOqXo7IIaQCqz90R2IeGDfUVpyZq6F0VsHhYmgJ0-Ckgt3ZcneYIUkB-A6J-UQaLk8MLRp7gasyi5Eroh8zb89-Kv4HOwRHxvtHhsirOCLWzeVDa35KPqrCldUosXzv0Duq7y1Nq9haS9rWkthlKpqsHuDsCnVNbI9w44ra3r4B-fahO7EKoLRTsNK9rTO&c=fJQnG0_lnIrAE9XIMdlvA3NLWe8GF3v1Y4Ugq8eMavfIMyXvMNaLAw==&ch=1vL4i9ToKLjaMZdoDM7PuiEXyXlsUcXUB9av-ianL5uk1c5b0EPNlg==)
---

clean_body: Say goodbye to Dems ever holding Congress again. | | | | | == | Peter, In 72 days, the GOP are on track to seize the Pennsylvania State Supreme Court in an obscure judicial election. The consequences of failing to act here are immense. | == | | | | Even though states like California are stepping up to retaliate to Trump's redistricting push, Republicans hold the advantage, controlling state legislatures and governorships of 23 states, compared with 15 for Democrats. So if the gerrymandering war continues to play out and every Red state and every Blue states draws new hyperpartisan maps, Democrats lose. This is why the newest front in this fight is coming down to swing state supreme courts. MAGA tried earlier this year to take the Supreme Court in Wisconsin but was repelled and now they are trying to do the same thing in PA this November. Republican capture of the court in PA would be a checkmate blow for democrats, a coup de grace. We rely on PA for seven house seats and are on track to pick up two to four red PA house seats if trends continue and Democrats sweep the upcoming midterms. Losing this court means that Pennsylvania's vulnerable red seats will be shored up and all of our blue seats will be erased just like they were in Texas. Say goodbye to Dems ever holding Congress again. == | | | | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | | | | == In these final 72 days until this critical election we can sit back in apathy and watch as we lose our Democracy. name: Adam Schiff
subject: Trump’s “lock him up” rhetoric has become real
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[ unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T79ac5c7c-b70d-4ca4-9233-6da5b605dfc1/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _._
---
|
---
| | |
---
| | | | | Peter — this is Adam Schiff. Blue Amp Action said I could reach out with this urgent message: Donald Trump recently went on Truth Social and called for me to be prosecuted. Why? Because I led his first impeachment. Because I’ve held him accountable. Because I haven’t stopped. And I never will. This is just Trump’s latest attempt at political retaliation against his perceived enemies. [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9c5e3118-fba1-40a5-a6e8-8b2741cee2cb/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Over the years, Trump has called for me to be jailed for a variety of reasons, including accusing me of treason. And this latest baseless attempt at political retribution won’t stop me from holding him accountable — not by a long shot. I’m not surprised that Trump is lashing out again. But even by his standards, this one’s weak. And as much as Trump may hope, this latest smear will not distract from his Epstein files problem. [Nevertheless, I need your help right now to respond: rush a split donation between me and Blue Amp Action to help us fight back and keep holding Trump accountable.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T000ce571-857d-4013-bf4b-7fb50c886d8e/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
|
---
| | | | [DONATE $10 NOW →](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4a57f729-f5df-4c5b-a537-f30d48599fa0/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [DONATE $25 NOW →](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tdecf461e-ec7b-4146-9c18-4c168e15453a/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [DONATE MORE NOW →](https://default.salsalabs.org/T30c40722-d92e-45ac-9df6-86dc2fe94fa3/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Trump doesn’t want to talk about skyrocketing prices, his tariff failures, the big ugly bill or why his rabid supporters are demanding answers over his refusal to release the Epstein files. So he goes back to the same tired playbook: Attack, distract, repeat. But I won’t be intimidated. And I won’t stop doing my job – not now, not ever. Instead of working to help families hit by extreme weather, rising costs, or his own healthcare cuts, Trump is using his platform to lash out at those who tell the truth. He wants to silence his critics. But I refuse to back down – and with you in my corner, I never will. [Please, rush a split donation to help me and Blue Amp Action fight back today.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6dbf119b-54b1-43db-af1a-f9ceb66b1424/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Trump can lie and threaten me all he wants. I will never be deterred from fulfilling my duties as a U.S. Senator. Thank you for standing with me, Adam
---
|
---
| | |
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
| | | | Cliff Schecter President, Blue Amp Action Amplify Good
---|---
| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade, it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in a democracy, not a dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do!
---
|
---
| | Follow Us | [](https://twitter.com/intent/follow?screen_name=cliffschecter) | [](https://linkedin.com/in/cliffschecter-0873a448/)
---|---

1776 Mentor Ave. #411 | Norwood, Ohio 45212
513.438.0171 | info@mail.blueampaction.com

* * *
# [The Shape of the Economy](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=171837293&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxODM3MjkzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTYxMjMzMDMsImV4cCI6MTc1ODcxNTMwMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.w7v1Dzk3HNFfasgbVWMUQg1aCmnpH8POGz65WSi-quI)

---

Hello, I hope that the summer is ending on a high for you and yours.
The economy has been showing signs of slowing recently. How is it going? It depends on where you're situated.
Heather Long, an economics reporter, recently posted this graph of consumption patterns in the U.S.:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fe622d3a-a6ed-474a-b103-5116f0bb0344?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
As you can see, spending among the bottom 80% of the U.S. by income has pulled back; most Americans are now increasing spending at more or less the same rate as inflation. The top 20% though, has been largely resilient up until now and continues to spend. This is forming "The K-Shaped Economy" as you have different groups behaving differently, forming a visible 'K' in the graph above as one group goes up and the other goes down.
What can you say about the top 20% of Americans by income? They're older. They tend to hold stock and investments. They likely own a home and maybe other real estate. They rely less upon their job for income, or their job might be resistant to shocks (e.g., an orthodontist or eye doctor). Meanwhile, their stock holdings and home value have appreciated meaningfully, so they may feel pretty flush.
The bottom 80% of course describes the vast majority of Americans. Most are [struggling](https://substack.com/redirect/8ebd7957-4544-479f-b202-d5feab746cec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and essentially live paycheck-to-paycheck. Job security is getting tougher and tougher, and losing their job is an enormous blow if it does happen. I recently heard of a major company pushing over a thousand employees into early retirement - a friend was among those [laid off](https://substack.com/redirect/8ebd7957-4544-479f-b202-d5feab746cec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Meanwhile, most Americans' costs have been creeping up relentlessly, and will go up again as the Trump tariffs are fully implemented. Young people are faced with a job market that is already being [transformed](https://substack.com/redirect/b0a2e4c8-f3c0-46aa-9df7-30b2e57d9ac7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by AI. A recent stat said that 70-year olds are now more likely to be buying a house than 35-year olds. That's not what you want to see.
Signs are mounting that the water is getting rough. Loan delinquency levels are rising and consumer confidence is [weakening](https://substack.com/redirect/1a812cba-a004-4418-b10d-b1a0922ac120?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Visitors to Las Vegas dropped 11.3% in June, among both domestic and foreign travelers. Foreign tourists, particularly Canadians, are visiting the U.S. less, which will result in $12.5 billion less spending in New York City, California, Vegas and other destinations this year, another result of Trump's trade war.
Where will it go from here? I'm pessimistic about the real economy as experienced by most people, as I think the pressures are set to increase, including AI-fueled [layoffs](https://substack.com/redirect/b0a2e4c8-f3c0-46aa-9df7-30b2e57d9ac7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and workers quietly not being replaced, as well as companies passing along tariffs in the form of higher prices.
The stock market has been frothy for a while now, but corporate efficiencies are set to spike so it's hard to say what's to come. I've spoken to numerous insiders who say their profitability will go up as their headcount goes down. We're witnessing a trillion dollar investment in AI and data centers by various tech companies whose valuations are being rewarded by investors the more they spend. What's bad for workers may be good for shareholders, and the economy and the stock market may walk different paths.
This is fundamentally the theme of the K-Shaped economy - the economy is diverging along various lines depending on where you're sitting. The winner-take-all economy is becoming all the more extreme as AI supercharges the power and importance of having the most capital, data and processing power. The scale of some of these changes is beyond the ability of most people to comprehend. I was recently at an AI conference and the computer scientist Geoffrey Hinton suggested that AI is certain to dominate our lives, so the goal should be to give it maternal instincts so that it wants to look after us. Yikes. At the same conference, I argued that we may be nearing a point where there is more than enough to go around - a point of abundance - as AI ratchets up productivity and innovation and grows the economy. GDP per capita is at $85k per head and set to rise. We could do big things for people as we leave scarcity behind.
One thing I used to point out to the wealthy among us when I was running for President is that satisfaction levels are higher in a more equal society regardless of where you are on the curve. It's no fun being in a vastly unequal society. What good is abundance if most people are experiencing the opposite?
Can the upcoming abundance be shared? We are a very long way away from that right now.
To see what Forward is doing in your area, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/ddad8b95-e222-48c4-a02a-558d327b9a85?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). For my interview with David Jolly who is running for Governor of Florida, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/2be52a79-df99-417c-9f40-f6b66d1a4805?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The next [Offline Party](https://substack.com/redirect/ac34e1b6-00a7-47d8-9136-08c830175844?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is in New York on September 18th. I'm working on something that will hopefully get more buying power into people's hands. Look up.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/74fbbc84-902b-45dc-a3b3-ef7d35f2ac2d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

body:
Reach a service member today with care packages and more critical support.
[](https://www.uso.org)
[MATCH MY GIFT](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/4NKwHK-W_Wfb/YWNUb2tZN3Z3djJLa2RVdllFRVdPa3hLVFBhV0xuOExMSWZqMGk3R29tSGF0dGcyKzVYb3lmUGRUeTg5SnRqU3hMTkpSbHJPdDNqZEJwWncxKzhXeXYwUXBBNUVVV05rNThQNDMra3hRTVU9S0/T3d2ZkI4dkJZR3BlM1lJdFZQdnhZUjNiYlhmanhPeDZSQ1BuQ2QySjNQbTBKSU8zSmU4RkZLV3FQVCtacElVMDB6UjFSSXBRVmdFUFNjL1NGT0RGeGZXQjh6RjRwK0c3S0)

USO Supporter,
When our service members are missing home and precious moments with their loved ones, hearing from caring military supporters like you can make all the difference. **_" It's a small reminder that no matter where you are in your military journey, or where you are stationed, the USO will always bring a little home to you," said a service member stationed in Guam._**
**This is the last day of this match opportunity, so please rush your gift now.[Remember, every dollar of your support will be matched,* up to a total of $40,000**, to provide care packages and other critical support for our service members.](https://links.e.uso.)**
You'll show the heroes bravely serving our nation that you appreciate all they do to protect our country and freedoms.
| | |
---|---|---
| [ YOUR CARE PACKAGE ](https://links.e.uso.) [ SUPPORTER RECORD ](https://links.e.uso.) [ MILITARY SUPPORTER: ](https://links.e.uso.) [YES](https://links.e.uso.) [ MATCHED GIFT: ](https://links.e.uso.) [PENDING](https://links.e.uso.) [ MATCH DEADLINE: ](https://links.e.uso.) [MIDNIGHT](https://links.e.uso.) |
| |
**[DOUBLE YOUR GIFT NOW](https://links.e.uso.)**
---
We checked our records and noticed you haven't had the chance to have your gift matched towards care packages and other support. **[It takes just a moment to give, and your generous gift of any amount DOUBLES today only to go 2X farther to help!](https://links.e.uso.)**
Thank you for supporting our nation's heroes.
With gratitude,
The USO
**[MATCH MY GIFT](https://links.e.uso.)**
---
* A generous group of USO donors is matching all gifts to the USO, dollar for dollar, up to a total of $40,000, through August 25, 2025.
**Your contribution will be joined with the contributions of others and used where it is needed most to strengthen the well-being of the people serving in America's military and their families.

name: Matt Royer from By the Ballot
subject: Tough on What Crime Exactly?
body:
Is our politics of fear dominating our reality?
