[](https://substack.com/redirect/84ade46e-2504-457b-a935-48541a8d11f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_How J.B Pritzker must react to any Trump troops in Chicago_
## Trump's War on Chicago Is a War on Democracy
[](https://substack.com/redirect/3d85a602-6aa5-4d6d-9b06-271ae8113463?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_As is clear, when we saw Linkin Park in Chicago two weeks ago, chaos(!), violence everywhere!_
* * *
Friends, Donald Trump isn't interested in crime, unless you mean finding more ways to commit it.
The minute the 34-count felon and adjudicated rapist with the three other indictments for numerous counts and 20 something sexual-assault accusers showed up in D.C., the percentage of criminals in The District went up.
And we're not even including whatever Don Jr. is on when he's in town and livestreaming with eyes more bloodshot than Antietam.
Trump has a new plan to show America just how the "law and order" of a criminal works: he's been bragging to anyone stuck listening that he will send U.S. troops into Chicago.
Governor J.B. Pritzker, who's been outspoken on what a fascist prick Trump is, wants no part of it. Neither does Chicago Mayor, Brandon Johnson. And why would they?
Contrary to the lies spewed by Trump and his racist cabal of weathercocks, homicides in Chicago were down by over 30%, shootings 40% over the past year.
In much the same way [crime in Washington, D.C. is at a 30-year low. Yet Trump, for a pretext to send in the National Guard,](https://substack.com/redirect/fca3643e-41d6-42ce-8b1c-dbe0b9837418?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) pretended its violence was as rampant as his lifetime exposure to venereal disease.
But, this would only matter if what Trump is doing--as well as what he plans to do in 19 red states--had anything to do with crime. Of course it doesn't.
Meanwhile, Pritzker and other Illinois officials also made it clear that it is illegal for him to send in troops:
## "JB Pritzker emphatically dismissed Trump's warning, saying the president 'has absolutely no right and no legal ability to send troops in to the city of Chicago.'"

First, there's that whole "posse comitatus" thing that bars presidents from deploying the military against their own citizens. Yes, Trump doesn't care, and he has already been shut down by courts for treating the Pentagon like his personal MAGA militia.
But, in addition, while D.C. has always (and shamefully) been kept in a state of constitutional limbo, Illinois has no such issue. From the _Chicago Sun Times_ :
> Even Fraternal Order of Police President John Catanzara, who has been supportive of Trump over the years, said the president doesn't have the same jurisdiction that he has in Washington D.C.
> "The president says a lot of things that aren't necessarily doable. They're more, I would say wishes or dreams," Catanzara said.
> "It's a different environment than it is in D.C. They do have federal jurisdiction in D.C. They do not here in the same realm. The National Guard in D.C. is his National Guard. It doesn't belong to anybody else... I can't even imagine a world -- short of an L.A. kind of riot situation -- where it would be legally feasible."
This is the same Catanzara who has been accused of falsifying police reports and posting racist messages on social media. And even this goon is like, uh, no, the a-hole can't do that.
But again, this is not about crime.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.9DoHkkN59Oy8q60yKbw3AANNiBR_pK6Pk5zYPAfR1fc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=171912799)
Trump wants tanks where there should be teachers, soldiers where there should be social workers. And anyone who thinks dropping the 82nd Airborne into a major American city ends peacefully, probably also believes Trump's cerebral matter isn't some amalgamation of delousing powder and a horseshoe crab using his skull as shelter.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6d07670f-624d-4f60-9cd6-6dfd86e8b06a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
So here's the real reason we're going through this trauma as a nation. One, control. Two, the Epstein files--[look over there everyone](https://substack.com/redirect/764e9829-b7e1-4061-9894-7a7e7911bfe4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)!
But three, and perhaps most important, always look at the data points and connect the dots. He is **the most unpopular President ever at this point**. The aforementioned Epstein files, the stupid TACO tariffs, destroying the lives of his own voters whether a rancher, government worker or small business owner.
Even immigration, with t[he cruelty of the images of kids being thrown to the ground](https://substack.com/redirect/b7965839-2897-4409-80c9-3405f23c0ad9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), deported while they have cancer, has become a losing issue for him, [unless you were the crappiest Superman of all time](https://substack.com/redirect/697ad5c3-8c2b-4850-b5b6-63723759d9fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
So do the math. If Trump had two issues he used based on racism to help him win in 2024, immigration and crime--both bs of course, his pipsqueak, porn-monitoring minion, Mike Johnson blocked a bipartisan immigration bill--and now immigration is gone, what does he have left?
He has to go all in on crime.
So, first, he sends in the National Guard to places like Chicago. The is a stunt **is guaranteed to escalate violence** , not calm it--because when you treat very specific citizens like enemy combatants, conflict has a way of erupting.
Then he can send out the images--in between brunch bites of the KFC Crispy Thigh Meal--and say "see, told you they're criminals. They're animals. We won't allow this." And if he does it in Chicago, well then he can do it in reddish/purple states with blue cities, like, I dunno, Ohio?
We have [Sherrod Brown running for an open Senate seat, as I wrote about recently](https://substack.com/redirect/50f81e91-725c-4e77-8461-a6f58bdc2344?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and he only lost by 3% in 2024, over-performing the top of the ticket by 8%! Meaning, he's poised to win with Trump in the dumper and not on the ballot.
But what if Trump sends in the National Guard to pick fights, gets to show images of non-white violence and run on the message of he's fighting for you, Klan-adjacent-white-exurban guy. He's gonna stop those Blacks!
And then can use those same guardsman to threaten/scare Latino, Black and other "undesirables" into not voting. Then Trump can, with the help of gerrymandering and every single other way he can cheat, hang on even if he has a 35% approval rating.
I've already heard the messaging, sadly, repeated by people I know. And I've seen it, too, as you have.
It's why when there was a brawl outside a nightclub in Cincinnati, the **two worst residents of Cincinnati of all time** --and Charles Manson is from here--Vivek Ramaswamy and J.D. Vance, spread it nationally, as among the victimes, was a white woman, and among the attackers, Black men. Wink-wink.
.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.9DoHkkN59Oy8q60yKbw3AANNiBR_pK6Pk5zYPAfR1fc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=171912799)
This is why you look for the dots to connect, because unlike the message-challenged DNC, the wholly **Beelzebub** Republican Party coordinates their messaging. Nothing is just said or written without purpose. I've heard this garbage because it's been on their email lists, talk radio, Fox "News."
The point is they're building a narrative with right-wing media. And they know c[orporate media with just repeat it without a second thought](https://substack.com/redirect/2dc232f1-1b15-4539-a4ad-924955d6bedc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
And we cannot let them get away with it, we must resist by, as Malcolm X said, "any means necessary (I'll add non-violently, unless in self-defense).
Now think of swing states or seats with GOP Governors, and how they may take this strategy on a road trip to Atlanta, Savannah, Jacksonville, Miami, Tampa, New Orleans, Houston, Dallas, Austin and on and on. See my point?
They get to try to trick Americans into thinking crime is out of control by baiting violence, and suppress voters with any number of lies about illegality or violent attacks on election day--or perhaps they won't give any reason at all.
Illinois and Chicago leaders are denouncing this power grab because they can see it for what it is: Trump consolidating power the way every strongman does--by manufacturing crisis, then using it to justify crushing dissent and halting a fair vote.
It's the same playbook Putin, Erdoğan, and yes, our orange-hued friend's very own matinee idol, Viktor Orban, have run with sickening success.
Today, it's Chicago. Tomorrow, coming to a city near you. This is the creeping normalization of a domestic occupation force. If Democrats don't shout this from the rooftops, they're handing Trump the keys to authoritarian rule with a bow on top.
For example, if Trump wants to stop crime in Chicago, they may wanna SPEAK UP about the **longest serving GOP House Speaker,** **Denny Hastert, who lived in prison for not nearly enough time for molesting teenage boys.** Or Republican 2022 Senate candidate, Allison Salinas, recently arrested for actually planning a hit (because, MAGA!).
Or wirth respect to Trump's clear worry about crime in general, besides not having self-arrested, maybe ask him [why he hasn't sent troops in](https://substack.com/redirect/fca3643e-41d6-42ce-8b1c-dbe0b9837418?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to watch the 1,600 January 6th terrorists he pardoned, including one now advising the DOJ, who was caught on film encouraging his terrorist pals to murder cops.
Or how about Paul Manafort? Roger Stone? Dinesh D'Souza? Peter Navarro? Tom Barrack? Rick Gates? Michael Flynn? Maybe look into the Pete Hegseth settlement with the women he was accused of assaulting?
If Trump's really worried about crime, there are a lot of folks all around him who have been accused of it--even convicted. He could start with them! Alas, again, it has nothing to do with crime, as nothing Trump ever does has anything to do with what he claims it does.
Part of the whole [being a narcissistic, sociopathic, congenital-liar thing](https://substack.com/redirect/4f09cfb0-971f-4d0a-b3f9-ab04d5da43cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
So, my friends, to be clear, this isn't just another Trump tantrum. It's a trial balloon for American fascism. Every time he ignores the law and flexes the military at us like his own private security firm, he's daring us to get used to it--and what it will in turn do to us, and democracy.
And if we do just get used to it, we'll wake up in a country someday soon where the military enforces political loyalty instead of protecting the nation.
Trump has always been a wannabe tyrant. Sending troops into Chicago just proves he's willing to make it official.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.9DoHkkN59Oy8q60yKbw3AANNiBR_pK6Pk5zYPAfR1fc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=171912799)

_**[LET'S GET AMPED UP!](https://substack.com/redirect/7471369e-d941-483d-a78f-4cd8b13b7b4b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
[](https://substack.com/redirect/6e904a60-740d-42e5-88ad-ef7e62aded44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/0cb3745f-fbb7-4054-9587-7f2324336acd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | |

clean_body: Why don't you ask the kids at Tiananmen Square? Was fashion the reason why they were there? They disguise it, hypnotize it. Television made you buy it - System of a Down, "Hypnotize" | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Why don't you ask the kids at Tiananmen Square? Was fashion the reason why they were there? They disguise it, hypnotize it. Television made you buy it - System of a Down, "Hypnotize" | | | Aug 25 | | | | | | | | | | | | THE BLUE LETTER | | ---|---|--- Monopolies/billionaires are trying to suffocate democracy. To celebrate workers on the front lines of the fight, we're offering How J.B Pritzker must react to any Trump troops in Chicago Trump's War on Chicago Is a War on Democracy As is clear, when we saw Linkin Park in Chicago two weeks ago, chaos(!), violence everywhere! Friends, Donald Trump isn't interested in crime, unless you mean finding more ways to commit it. The minute the 34-count felon and adjudicated rapist with the three other indictments for numerous counts and 20 something sexual-assault accusers showed up in D.C., the percentage of criminals in The District went up. And we're not even including whatever Don Jr. is on when he's in town and livestreaming with eyes more bloodshot than Antietam. Trump has a new plan to show America just how the "law and order" of a criminal works: he's been bragging to anyone stuck listening that he will send U.S. troops into Chicago. Governor J.B. Pritzker, who's been outspoken on what a fascist prick Trump is, wants no part of it. Neither does Chicago Mayor, Brandon Johnson. And why would they? Contrary to the lies spewed by Trump and his racist cabal of weathercocks, homicides in Chicago were down by over 30%, shootings 40% over the past year. In much the same way pretended its violence was as rampant as his lifetime exposure to venereal disease. But, this would only matter if what Trump is doing--as well as what he plans to do in 19 red states--had anything to do with crime. Of course it doesn't. Meanwhile, Pritzker and other Illinois officials also made it clear that it is illegal for him to send in troops: "JB Pritzker emphatically dismissed Trump's warning, saying the president 'has absolutely no right and no legal ability to send troops in to the city of Chicago.'" First, there's that whole "posse comitatus" thing that bars presidents from deploying the military against their own citizens. Yes, Trump doesn't care, and he has already been shut down by courts for treating the Pentagon like his personal MAGA militia. But, in addition, while D.C. has always (and shamefully) been kept in a state of constitutional limbo, Illinois has no such issue. From the Chicago Sun Times : Even Fraternal Order of Police President John Catanzara, who has been supportive of Trump over the years, said the president doesn't have the same jurisdiction that he has in Washington D.C. "The president says a lot of things that aren't necessarily doable. They're more, I would say wishes or dreams," Catanzara said. "It's a different environment than it is in D.C. They do have federal jurisdiction in D.C. They do not here in the same realm. The National Guard in D.C. is his National Guard. It doesn't belong to anybody else... I can't even imagine a world -- short of an L.A. kind of riot situation -- where it would be legally feasible." This is the same Catanzara who has been accused of falsifying police reports and posting racist messages on social media. And even this goon is like, uh, no, the a-hole can't do that. But again, this is not about crime. Trump wants tanks where there should be teachers, soldiers where there should be social workers. And anyone who thinks dropping the 82nd Airborne into a major American city ends peacefully, probably also believes Trump's cerebral matter isn't some amalgamation of delousing powder and a horseshoe crab using his skull as shelter. | | ---|---|--- So here's the real reason we're going through this trauma as a nation. One, control. Two, the Epstein files--! But three, and perhaps most important, always look at the data points and connect the dots. He is the most unpopular President ever at this point. The aforementioned Epstein files, the stupid TACO tariffs, destroying the lives of his own voters whether a rancher, government worker or small business owner. Even immigration, with t, deported while they have cancer, has become a losing issue for him, . So do the math. If Trump had two issues he used based on racism to help him win in 2024, immigration and crime--both bs of course, his pipsqueak, porn-monitoring minion, Mike Johnson blocked a bipartisan immigration bill--and now immigration is gone, what does he have left? He has to go all in on crime. So, first, he sends in the National Guard to places like Chicago. The is a stunt is guaranteed to escalate violence , not calm it--because when you treat very specific citizens like enemy combatants, conflict has a way of erupting. Then he can send out the images--in between brunch bites of the KFC Crispy Thigh Meal--and say "see, told you they're criminals. They're animals. We won't allow this." And if he does it in Chicago, well then he can do it in reddish/purple states with blue cities, like, I dunno, Ohio? We have , and he only lost by 3% in 2024, over-performing the top of the ticket by 8%! Meaning, he's poised to win with Trump in the dumper and not on the ballot. But what if Trump sends in the National Guard to pick fights, gets to show images of non-white violence and run on the message of he's fighting for you, Klan-adjacent-white-exurban guy. He's gonna stop those Blacks! And then can use those same guardsman to threaten/scare Latino, Black and other "undesirables" into not voting. Then Trump can, with the help of gerrymandering and every single other way he can cheat, hang on even if he has a 35% approval rating. I've already heard the messaging, sadly, repeated by people I know. And I've seen it, too, as you have. It's why when there was a brawl outside a nightclub in Cincinnati, the two worst residents of Cincinnati of all time --and Charles Manson is from here--Vivek Ramaswamy and J.D. Vance, spread it nationally, as among the victimes, was a white woman, and among the attackers, Black men. Wink-wink. . This is why you look for the dots to connect, because unlike the message-challenged DNC, the wholly Beelzebub Republican Party coordinates their messaging. Nothing is just said or written without purpose. I've heard this garbage because it's been on their email lists, talk radio, Fox "News." The point is they're building a narrative with right-wing media. And they know c. And we cannot let them get away with it, we must resist by, as Malcolm X said, "any means necessary (I'll add non-violently, unless in self-defense). Now think of swing states or seats with GOP Governors, and how they may take this strategy on a road trip to Atlanta, Savannah, Jacksonville, Miami, Tampa, New Orleans, Houston, Dallas, Austin and on and on. See my point? They get to try to trick Americans into thinking crime is out of control by baiting violence, and suppress voters with any number of lies about illegality or violent attacks on election day--or perhaps they won't give any reason at all. Illinois and Chicago leaders are denouncing this power grab because they can see it for what it is: Trump consolidating power the way every strongman does--by manufacturing crisis, then using it to justify crushing dissent and halting a fair vote. It's the same playbook Putin, Erdoğan, and yes, our orange-hued friend's very own matinee idol, Viktor Orban, have run with sickening success. Today, it's Chicago. Tomorrow, coming to a city near you. This is the creeping normalization of a domestic occupation force. If Democrats don't shout this from the rooftops, they're handing Trump the keys to authoritarian rule with a bow on top. For example, if Trump wants to stop crime in Chicago, they may wanna SPEAK UP about the longest serving GOP House Speaker, Denny Hastert, who lived in prison for not nearly enough time for molesting teenage boys. Or Republican 2022 Senate candidate, Allison Salinas, recently arrested for actually planning a hit (because, MAGA!). Or wirth respect to Trump's clear worry about crime in general, besides not having self-arrested, maybe ask him to watch the 1,600 January 6th terrorists he pardoned, including one now advising the DOJ, who was caught on film encouraging his terrorist pals to murder cops. Or how about Paul Manafort? Roger Stone? Dinesh D'Souza? Peter Navarro? Tom Barrack? Rick Gates? Michael Flynn? Maybe look into the Pete Hegseth settlement with the women he was accused of assaulting? If Trump's really worried about crime, there are a lot of folks all around him who have been accused of it--even convicted. He could start with them! Alas, again, it has nothing to do with crime, as nothing Trump ever does has anything to do with what he claims it does. Part of the whole . So, my friends, to be clear, this isn't just another Trump tantrum. It's a trial balloon for American fascism. Every time he ignores the law and flexes the military at us like his own private security firm, he's daring us to get used to it--and what it will in turn do to us, and democracy. And if we do just get used to it, we'll wake up in a country someday soon where the military enforces political loyalty instead of protecting the nation. Trump has always been a wannabe tyrant. Sending troops into Chicago just proves he's willing to make it official. | | | | | , , and | | * ---|---|--- | Aug 14 | Monopolies/billionaires are trying to suffocate democracy. body:
Can I have a few moments of your attention? |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113986775/565571210/823540626?refcode=ea.d5232046-0282-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.d5232046-0282-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTIzMjA0Ni0wMjgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=w-jY876Hm6xIXDJjmlM_IqzxLRYgJsGD061khVW8Vbw=&emci=d4232046-0282-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=d5232046-0282-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=17205535)
Hi Peter, it’s Pete Buttigieg. Roy Cooper and I share a basic belief: the status quo is not serving most Americans. While families are paying more at the grocery store, too many in Washington are busy delivering tax breaks for the wealthiest instead of making life more affordable for everyone. We have to do better. And we can. One of the best chances we’ll have to change course will come next year, as Democrats organize to take back Congress. And the path to a new Senate majority runs straight through North Carolina. **That’s why I’m supporting Roy in this critical 2026 U.S. Senate race — and why I’m asking you to join me today.** Roy is the only Democrat who can win this race, and he has proven that he’s the best choice for North Carolina. I know this because I’ve worked with him — when he was Governor and I was Secretary of Transportation — and I’ve seen firsthand how deeply he is committed to doing the right thing, building coalitions that include people from every walk of life and every corner of the state. Roy doesn’t just work for the people who agree with him. He works for everyone. Roy is deeply respected, but North Carolina is always one of the most competitive states in the country. He can’t win this fight alone, and he’ll need the resources to stay competitive through the long months ahead. **[So I’m personally asking you: Will you please make an investment in Roy’s campaign today? A donation of any size will make a difference in this race.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113986776/565571211/-1743631236?refcode=ea.d5232046-0282-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.d5232046-0282-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTIzMjA0Ni0wMjgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=w-jY876Hm6xIXDJjmlM_IqzxLRYgJsGD061khVW8Vbw=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $5](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113986777/565571212/-1743631236?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=ea.d5232046-0282-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.d5232046-0282-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTIzMjA0Ni0wMjgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=w-jY876Hm6xIXDJjmlM_IqzxLRYgJsGD061khVW8Vbw=)

clean_body: Can I have a few moments of your attention? | Hi Peter, it’s Pete Buttigieg. Roy Cooper and I share a basic belief: the status quo is not serving most Americans. While families are paying more at the grocery store, too many in Washington are busy delivering tax breaks for the wealthiest instead of making life more affordable for everyone. We have to do better. And we can. One of the best chances we’ll have to change course will come next year, as Democrats organize to take back Congress. And the path to a new Senate majority runs straight through North Carolina. That’s why I’m supporting Roy in this critical 2026 U.S. Senate race — and why I’m asking you to join me today. Roy is the only Democrat who can win this race, and he has proven that he’s the best choice for North Carolina. I know this because I’ve worked with him — when he was Governor and I was Secretary of Transportation — and I’ve seen firsthand how deeply he is committed to doing the right thing, building coalitions that include people from every walk of life and every corner of the state. Roy doesn’t just work for the people who agree with him. He works for everyone. Roy is deeply respected, but North Carolina is always one of the most competitive states in the country. He can’t win this fight alone, and he’ll need the resources to stay competitive through the long months ahead. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = = = = = = Roy has my full support. I hope he can count on yours, too. Thanks, Pete Buttigieg = Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here. National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us. Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party. Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy. = = = mailto:info@roycooper.com | Cooper for North Carolina PO Box 1190 Raleigh, NC 27602 Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina Contributions or gifts to Cooper for North Carolina are not tax deductible. name: Brad Finstad
subject: Fateh & Socialism in Minneapolis.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Finstad for Congress
body:
| | | | |
---
| Dear Friend, The radical left is on the march in Minnesota. Just look at what’s happening in Minneapolis: Democrat Socialists like Omar Fateh are working hand-in-hand with city leaders who openly attack anyone who doesn’t fall in line with their far-left agenda. Recently, the City Council Vice President was even caught on camera shouting “F--- [Minneapolis Mayor] Jacob Frey” — not because he’s conservative, but because he wasn’t radical enough for their socialist revolution. This is what today’s Democrat Party has become. Mob politics. Profanity-laced tirades. A dangerous embrace of socialism that is driving families, jobs, and public safety out of our communities. Make no mistake: their goal isn’t to take over Minneapolis. These radicals want to spread their agenda statewide and then into Washington D.C. They’re bankrolled by national socialist organizations and liberal billionaires who are eager to turn Minnesota into a testing ground for their failed policies. I’m fighting every day in Congress to stop them, to defend our conservative values, and to keep socialism where it belongs — in the dustbin of history. But I can’t do it alone. Will you chip in $10, $25, or even $50 today to help us push back against the socialist takeover of our cities?
---
| | [👉 Contribute Now](http://link.finstadforcongress.com/api/LinkHandler/getaction2?%3d)
---
| The choice is clear: socialism or freedom. Chaos or common sense. If we don’t stand strong now, the radicals will steamroll every city and every community across our state. Let’s send a message loud and clear: Minnesota rejects socialism. Gratefully,

Brad Finstad

name: Binkley for Congress
subject: You're Invited!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ryan Binkley for Congress
body:
Kickoff Rally September 14th

| | | | | |
---
| | Dear Friend,
I have exciting news—on Sunday, September 14th at 4:00 PM, we’ll officially launch my campaign for U.S. Congress with a Kickoff Rally at Create Church in Richardson.

This campaign is about restoring America’s economic and spiritual foundation. Together, we can secure the border, unleash American energy, restore prosperity, and protect the values that matter most to North Texas families.

I hope you will join me and my family as we begin this journey. Your presence will help set the tone for a strong campaign launch, and your early support will make all the difference in building the momentum we need to win.
RSVP today at [ Binkley Launch Eventbrite](https://binkleyforcongress.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a7930d2727540612917fd641e&id=a294fca183&e=a4fe331f26)
Create Church – Tent on Property
[3405 North Central Expressway](https://binkleyforcongress.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a7930d2727540612917fd641e&id=4e154995e2&e=a4fe331f26)
[Richardson, TX 75080](https://binkleyforcongress.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a7930d2727540612917fd641e&id=0793c43e36&e=a4fe331f26)
If you are unable to attend but want to stand with us from the very beginning, I would be grateful for your donation at [BinkleyforCongress.com](https://binkleyforcongress.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a7930d2727540612917fd641e&id=08676378bb&e=a4fe331f26). Every early gift sends a powerful message that this campaign is ready to win.

Thank you for your friendship, your prayers, and your support. I look forward to seeing you on September 14th!

With gratitude,
Ryan Binkley

name: Gaza UPDATE via Our Revolution
subject: Add your name: Tell the DNC to Act on Gaza TOMORROW
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo

Peter Willis,

Hours ago, Israeli forces bombed one of the main remaining hospitals in
Gaza – twice. First it struck surgical operating rooms at the clearly
marked medical facility. Then, as journalists and aid workers rushed in to
save the wounded, Israel struck a second time, killing those courageous
responders.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33693?source=email-20250825&t=1011&akid=9239%2E5885482%2EsJGi2G ]Headline: Israel strikes a Gaza hospital twice, killing at least 20,
including journalists and rescuers

It’s just the latest in a relentless, horrifying pattern of violence. And
it MUST stop.

Here’s our chance: the DNC is set to vote TOMORROW on a resolution to
suspend military aid to Israel. If it passes, it will send a clear, vital
message that enough is enough. Together let’s OVERWHELM DNC members with
support before the vote.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33693?source=email-20250825&t=1012&akid=9239%2E5885482%2EsJGi2G ]Add your name right now: Demand the DNC pass Resolution #18 to halt
U.S. weapons sales to Israel. With the vote happening in 24 hours, every
signature counts.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33693?source=email-20250825&t=1013&akid=9239%2E5885482%2EsJGi2G ]ADD YOUR NAME

Hospitals, refugee camps, humanitarian aid distribution sites: nowhere in
Gaza is safe. More than 60,000 Palestinians are dead – and Israel’s OWN
data shows 83 percent of them have been civilians. More than 200
journalists have been killed while desperately trying to expose the truth
and report on what our tax dollars make possible.

Yet, shamefully, the White House and Congress continue sending bombs and
bullets.

TOMORROW, DNC members have a chance to finally act. Resolution #18 draws a
clear line, calling for a recognition of a Palestinian state, a ceasefire,
an arms embargo, and a suspension of military aid to Israel.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33693?source=email-20250825&t=1014&akid=9239%2E5885482%2EsJGi2G ]Headline: DNC vote on Israel arms embargo may set Democratic Party's
course on Palestine

Our Revolution members are flooding the DNC with signatures to send a
powerful message: No more Democratic support for sending weapons to
Israel’s war on Gaza. No more blank checks for mass civilian casualties.
No more apologies and excuses.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33693?source=email-20250825&t=1015&akid=9239%2E5885482%2EsJGi2G ]Will you urgently take action now? Add your to demand that the DNC say
ENOUGH.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/33693?source=email-20250825&t=1016&akid=9239%2E5885482%2EsJGi2G ]ADD YOUR NAME

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Peter — Ron DeSantis is trying to dismantle the will of the people, and he’s doing it right out in the open. In 2010, Florida voters overwhelmingly passed two constitutional amendments to protect us from exactly what's happening right now: **politicians rigging district maps to silence our voices and choose their own voters.** But DeSantis doesn't care what Floridians voted for. He's bragging about redrawing congressional maps to help Donald Trump gain more seats in 2026. And our Attorney General – the person who should be defending our state constitution – is letting it happen. I've spent my career taking on corrupt politicians who think they're above the law, and I'm not about to stop now. As your next Attorney General, I'll fight to uphold every constitutional amendment Floridians pass – even when it's inconvenient for politicians in power. But I can't take on this corrupt system alone, Peter. **[Will you chip in $25 right now to help me become the People's Lawyer Florida needs? clean_body: That's not happening in Florida today. | Peter — Ron DeSantis is trying to dismantle the will of the people, and he’s doing it right out in the open. In 2010, Florida voters overwhelmingly passed two constitutional amendments to protect us from exactly what's happening right now: politicians rigging district maps to silence our voices and choose their own voters. But DeSantis doesn't care what Floridians voted for. He's bragging about redrawing congressional maps to help Donald Trump gain more seats in 2026. And our Attorney General – the person who should be defending our state constitution – is letting it happen. I've spent my career taking on corrupt politicians who think they're above the law, and I'm not about to stop now. As your next Attorney General, I'll fight to uphold every constitutional amendment Floridians pass – even when it's inconvenient for politicians in power. But I can't take on this corrupt system alone, Peter. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = = = = = Onward, Jose Javier Rodriguez | = = PAID FOR BY JOSE JAVIER RODRIGUEZ FOR FLORIDA ATTORNEY GENERAL Jose Javier Rodriguez for Attorney General 701 S. Howard Avenue #106-813 Tampa, FL 33606

name: Ryan Grim
subject: The New "Free Press" Editorial Defending its Famine Denial Includes Fundamental Errors
body:
I'm sure they'll issue a correction any moment

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..Utuwr1XWjPFTWwuVl57fcxScotedubkP0YPsWyGENb8?) for more
---
# [The New "Free Press" Editorial Defending its Famine Denial Includes Fundamental Errors](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1711&post_id=171930642&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxOTMwNjQyLCJpYXQiOjE3NTYxNjE0MTEsImV4cCI6MTc1ODc1MzQxMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE3MTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.NZOKewfEyODo9Cy9j9gyXKJb7pEFVIKmCJHKe1WQR90)
### I'm sure they'll issue a correction any moment
| | [Ryan Grim](https://substack.com/@ryangrim)
---
| Aug 25
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1711&post_id=171930642&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxOTMwNjQyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NjE2MTQxMSwiZXhwIjoxNzU4NzUzNDExLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTcxMSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Jr-T4NsX-F5wE8OWUAMnSFE9t0X7U2VwA42NQUqn6i4&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1711&post_id=171930642&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxOTMwNjQyLCJpYXQiOjE3NTYxNjE0MTEsImV4cCI6MTc1ODc1MzQxMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE3MTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.NZOKewfEyODo9Cy9j9gyXKJb7pEFVIKmCJHKe1WQR90&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1711&post_id=171930642&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxOTMwNjQyLCJpYXQiOjE3NTYxNjE0MTEsImV4cCI6MTc1ODc1MzQxMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE3MTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.NZOKewfEyODo9Cy9j9gyXKJb7pEFVIKmCJHKe1WQR90)
---
| | [](https://substack..bpRPr-5ptACRc4dt2kR2DhIUv5aOFBsJNV4cQF3Lskg?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/ryangrim/p/the-new-free-press-editorial-defending?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

The news outlet founded by Bari Weiss, The Free Press, [issued an editorial today ](https://substack.com/redirect/4671d943-bfe9-4091-96eb-c775ad87ea03?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)defending its recent reporting efforts that have sought to undermine claims that Gaza is facing a famine. The outlet's reporters have probed the medical histories of many of the children who have died or are dying of starvation and malnutrition and found that they also had pre-existing health complications.
Of course, the first to fall victim to a famine are obviously children who are already facing health difficulties. Cerebral Palsy, which The Free Press makes a big deal out of, is not fatal in children if treated. If they died with Cerebral Palsy, it probably wasn't Cerebral Palsy that killed them. It was the lack of treatment and the malnutrition. For a normal person, this would be obvious.
The outlet specifically complained that five people in particular had been unfair to them: myself, my Breaking Points colleagues Krystal Ball and Saagar Enjeti (Emily Jashinsky got a pass, apparently, though she's been critical of them too), Glenn Greenwald, and Pod Save America's Ben Rhodes, who they noted bore the nickname "Hamas" as an Obama administration official. (They forgot to note that it was AIPAC that called him that, not his colleagues.) They write:
> _To Krystal Ball, host of Breaking Points, our journalism was "just so disgusting." Ball's co-host, Saagar Enjeti, chimed in to compare our reporters to Holocaust deniers, saying that a "key tenet of holocaust denial is trying to claim that many of the initial victims or purported victims had other preconditions and that's part of the reason why they died."_
> _Those who care about the truth will note that these children were not presented as the initial victims of anything; they were deceptively promoted to reflect the average Gazan. To suggest otherwise betrays a fleeting relationship with reality._
> _Ball and Enjeti are not alone. Glenn Greenwald, a self-proclaimed "free speech absolutist," argued not just for our censorship, but for our "trial at the Hague."_
> _Barack Obama 's former deputy national security adviser, Ben Rhodes (whose nickname, incidentally, in that administration was "Hamas"), says we are "sociopathic." Ryan Grim, co-founder of Drop Site News, predicts reporter Olivia Reingold's "name will become notorious for a generation."_
They then repeatedly assert that they are not propagandists but are journalists who are only concerned with accuracy. In the next breath, they parrot a flagrant lie from the Israeli government. Had they not spent half their editorial accusing us of getting things wrong and the other half celebrating their commitment to journalistic accuracy, I'd leave it there. But they did do that, so I won't.
They write this: "Why have these reporters ignored [credible reports](https://substack.com/redirect/59c3c50e-d6cd-426c-b9f4-3cd072434df5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the United Nations and its allied organizations themselves blocking the distribution of aid in Gaza? And why are they [twisting the truth](https://substack.com/redirect/b630d509-322f-4a5b-8ced-05d3006cf8c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about Hamas's theft of aid? Similarly, why have they ignored the fact that the United Nations-associated body that attempts to assess whether there is a famine [monkeyed with the metrics](https://substack.com/redirect/4b37dac7-6f02-4376-a0bb-033ba14e7323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for its assessment in Gaza?"
The link to "credible reports" goes to a Fox News report and is not remotely credible. The U.N. is not blocking aid. The link regarding Hamas theft of aid goes to a Free Press article. Even Israeli media admits Hamas is not looting aid. The IDF maintains 24/7 drone surveillance of every inch of the aid distribution routes. Any looting by Hamas would be trumpeted by the IDF.
Those first two half-hearted lies are barely offensive because nobody is expected to believe them. But the final lie is the most egregious, because it's aimed at a genuinely curious audience, and seeks to undermine the official declaration of man-made famine in Gaza.
If you click on "monkeyed with the metrics," you arrive at a column by Seth Mandel, who would probably not even take offense at being called a propagandist for Israel. His column links to the "Washington Free Beacon," which, again: same.
The notion that the [Famine Review Committee](https://substack.com/redirect/2b2b06b8-ac56-443f-990e-1a3112bb017d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) fudged its numbers originated with the Israeli X account, [which said publicly](https://substack.com/redirect/842c3b1e-c14d-4bb5-8ca6-b12358b89d93?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that when it came to Somalia and Sudan, the IPC used a standard of 30 percent of malnourishment, whereas with Gaza, they used 15 percent, relying on a measurement known as MUAC, for "Mid-Upper Arm Circumference."
My [colleague Ball has a much more detailed](https://substack.com/redirect/aad8331d-a715-42d6-8890-726e06a97a44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) look at this claim today, but simply put, the Israeli military was lying, and the Free Beacon, Seth Mandel, and the Free Press either never checked, didn't care, or fact-checked the claim and still published a lie.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2b6a88c4-1a78-480d-9637-6d185deef8d5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
All we have to do is go read the Sudan report. To determine famine, as Krystal explains, officials prefer to take a detailed population survey of height and weight. But famines are not always found in ideal conditions; in such cases, measuring the depleting circumference of people's arms is a substitute. Because the arm withers at a slower rate than the overall body, seeing a collapse of arm circumference is more alarming, which is why the threshold is set at 15 percent rather than 30 for that measurement.
And reading the Sudan report, we find that the IPC indeed relied on MUAC: "The FRC relied on a Rapid Nutrition Assessment (RNA) conducted by MSF/Epicentre in Zamzam camp in January 2024, which also used in its August 2024 review. This assessment, using MUAC, reported GAM at 23.1 percent." The [term MUAC shows up](https://substack.com/redirect/0fd56b93-f6c2-40e3-a1f0-a041854ff127?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) more than two dozen times in the Sudan report from 2024. It [was also used to determine famine in the 2020 South Sudan report, ](https://substack.com/redirect/7ed02f10-74e5-4a06-8d95-b43b0c8e1259?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)and that report even includes a discussion of why MUAC numbers are lower, by a range of 7 to 15 percentage points, than height/weight numbers. Israel never complained in 2020 when this metric was applied to South Sudan. MUAC shows up more than a dozen times in [the Somalia report](https://substack.com/redirect/216a710e-2734-44dc-a18c-9553438e7084?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), as well. It is not new to the Gaza report.
The Free Press editorial finishes with a flourish of sanctimony.
> _For the Gaza information warriors, it 's not enough to acknowledge that there is hunger in Gaza. No. It must be premeditated genocide. Introducing facts into the discourse that complicate that judgment is condemned as genocide denial. Whatever one wants to call this kind of discourse, it's certainly not the work of journalists._
> _Ball gives the game away in her segment on Breaking Points. She asks at one point, after quoting our piece: "Is that supposed to be a propaganda win for Israel?"_
> _No. It 's just reporting the context that the mainstream, and the serially online YouTubers, leave out lest their narratives disintegrate and their audiences evaporate along with them. In other words, it is journalism._
A rule of thumb is that there is generally an inverse relationship between how loudly someone tells you that they are honest, and how honest they truly are. The more times a propagandist tells you they are a journalist, and that their work most certainly "is journalism," the more skeptical you should be. [Today's fairly short "Free Press" editorial](https://substack.com/redirect/4671d943-bfe9-4091-96eb-c775ad87ea03?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) touts their journalistic bona fides at least five or six times, while twice in the piece - seriously, twice! - asserting that their reporters "performed a public service." It's quite a performance, at least that much is true.
_This is my personal newsletter. To get my work at Drop Site News,[subscribe for free here.](https://substack.com/redirect/947d51b7-3bff-4154-a687-03e346fbd927?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_

| | Peter,

I’m launching my campaign in Napa, and **I’d be honored if you could be there.** This is about starting strong and building momentum for the months ahead.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113987325/565572887/-1244486190?source=EM_NBI_MT_20250825_INV_kickoff2_0_3MO_MikeThompson_NA_NA_kickoff2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDQ0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiOWNjMGUyNy0wNDgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aaGEoib_-ELbg3YfVivaUFQkfTC57T_UDTfpC8l0qKw=&emci=2befab90-0082-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=b9cc0e27-0482-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=2678920) **When: Saturday, September 13th, 11:00 AM — 12:00 PM
Where: Oxbow Commons, 1268 McKinstry Street, Napa, CA 94559**

_**“SHE WILL NOT LET YOU DOWN”**_ Peter, in the critical upcoming special election for Florida’s U.S. Senate seat, I am honored to tell you that **I just received President Trump’s total and complete endorsement!** **My name is Ashley Moody, and I’ve been serving in the U.S. Senate on a temporary basis since Marco Rubio became our top diplomat – _but now it’s time to win this seat outright._** I’m a fifth-generation Floridian, former judge and Attorney General, wife of a law enforcement officer, and mother to two wonderful boys. We can win this seat, but to be honest, history is stacked against us. **Typically, the president’s party averages a FOUR-SEAT LOSS in Senate midterm elections. If that happens, say hello to Majority Leader Chuck Schumer and goodbye to any forward movement on President Trump’s agenda.** _I don’t need to tell you what a disaster that would be for this country._ [**President Trump put his faith in me to win this critical special election, and now I’d like to ask for yours. name: Team Brown
subject: Trump is in Putin’s pocket.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress
body:
Do you need more evidence than this?

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113987919/565574783/1806897946?refcode=EM_F_250825Pocket_TB_90DO_5_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2UzMzMzYi0wNzgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ei5gxBZR2CCzr8fWuVjxbtyBUEjnHx8oAcmVGSSEa-w=) Trump is attacking our democracy...again.

He has stated that he is going to **lead the movement to “get rid of MAIL-IN BALLOTS,”** which he **falsely claims** are used by Democrats to cheat. I don’t know about you, but we are sick and tired of the Trump administration doing everything in its power to spread mass disinformation and to chip away at our election systems. **We have had ENOUGH.**

As reported by _CNN_ , the very idea of mail-in ballots being corrupt has been planted in Trump’s head by his best friend, Putin, who told him that he lost the 2020 election because of mail-in voting. Once again, Trump is being easily swayed by his favorite dictator at the expense of the American people.

We must join together to stand up against tyranny. Can we count on your support?

[**We are not going to sit back and let a wannabe king manipulate our elections. Team Brown | Shontel Brown is proud to be elected as the Congresswoman for Ohio’s 11th District. She has spent the last decade serving the people of Ohio and building coalitions. As she enters her second full term in Congress, she will use that experience to continue the fight for affordable quality health care, jobs that pay fair wages, a strong public education system, and to preserve and protect our democracy for generations to come. Will you join her and be the change we need?

name: David Hogg
subject: The generation we represent
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
We can only keep up the fight with your support.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20250825-1&t=1&akid=2574%2E587540%2EO9Lo7W)
Peter, I hear the same kind of frustrated reaction to Democratic fundraising a lot:
**_“Why are you asking me for $5 when Trump is destroying our country and Democrats aren’t doing anything about it?”_**
I can’t speak for every group, but I do know what sets Leaders We Deserve apart.
We represent a generation of people who walked out of their schools, marching with millions of their peers to demand action on gun violence and mass shootings.
Young people are at the forefront of progressive movements, fighting for solutions on issues like gun reform, climate change, student debt, and Gaza, to name a few.
Now, these same young leaders are old enough to actually run for political office, right at the time when we need a massive movement of young people in power.
**Leaders We Deserve exists to transform these movements into political power.** Running for office is more expensive than ever — and these young leaders need our support to help lead their movements into state legislatures and Congress. We know that the billionaire class isn’t showing up to bankroll these campaigns, so it’s not an exaggeration to say they can’t win these fights without grassroots support.
**[Peter, can you contribute $5 to power our transformational movement?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250825_1_transformation?refcode=em20250825-2574&amount=5&refcode2=2574_587540_O9Lo7W&akid=2574%2E587540%2EO9Lo7W)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250825_1_transformation?amount=5&refcode=em20250825-2574&refcode2=2574_587540_O9Lo7W&akid=2574%2E587540%2EO9Lo7W)**
**The young candidates that Leaders We Deserve supports are gamechangers. They will revitalize the Democratic Party and revolutionize our government.**
Our candidates are leading the fight to rebuild our political system. And they aren’t rebuilding the same political system that brought us school shootings, climate change, and everything that younger generations have to deal with in America.
They’re building something better — a system that actually _works_ for the people.
I believe that a big part of the answer to Donald Trump is building a movement of young people who will take back the reins of our government from the far right.
**Young people are up to this challenge.** If history has taught us anything, it's that great generations aren’t born, they’re made in the crucible of awful circumstances.
So when you give a donation to Leaders We Deserve, you aren’t just throwing out money at the status quo. You are investing in a different vision for what’s possible.
**[Please — pitch in $5 now to join our movement and fight for our future.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250825_1_transformation?refcode=em20250825-2574&amount=5&refcode2=2574_587540_O9Lo7W&akid=2574%2E587540%2EO9Lo7W)**
We can only do this work with your support. I deeply appreciate your generosity.
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20250825-1&t=2&refcode2=2574_587540_O9Lo7W&akid=2574%2E587540%2EO9Lo7W)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=2574%2E587540%2EO9Lo7W)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=2574%2E587540%2EO9Lo7W)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

name: Jay Jones
subject: Hard email to write
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Jones for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_new-gen-election-em_q32025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_new-gen-election-em_q32025]
Hey, SO sorry to bother you in the evening. This couldn't wait.
Our August fundraising has been rough. Our fundraising projections had us far outpacing where we’re at right now and the team is a little on edge.
Look, August is when campaigns usually lose a little steam. But that’s not true for my opponent. Jason Miyares is continuing to add on to his millions raised because MAGA billionaires are investing in this race, and we have no choice but to keep up our pace.
I hate asking on weeknights. I know you've got family time, errands, all that.
But we can't afford to take a single day off. Not when we're this close.
Miyares won by 0.81% last time. This race will come down to who fights harder right now, in August, when it matters most – and I’m ready to fight.
Can you help us catch up? Even $10 makes a difference. Click here to donate securely: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_new-gen-election-em_q32025?amount=10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_new-gen-election-em_q32025?amount=10] [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_new-gen-election-em_q32025&amount=10]
- Jay

name: AOC
subject: They're chickensh*t
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
Being an American is about being courageous.
#
This opposition that we’ve seen – all these billionaires that think they can buy our democracy — **all they do is tell people who to be afraid of**.
They’re scared of people coming with nothing but the shirt on their back and trying to start their American Dream.
They’re scared of women who own their body and tell them, “No.”
They’re scared of gay people, lesbians, and trans people. They’re scared of New York City and “childless cat ladies.” **They’re chickensh*t**.
To me, **being an American is about being courageous**.
When you ask people for the privilege of taking a role in leading this country, you have to stand up courageously and demand a better world for _all_ people.
You have to have the courage to say no to Wall Street and lobbyists and everyone trying to buy our government.
You have to have courage to know that an inclusive America is a better America _for everybody_.
While they run around and try to make everybody else as scared as they are, what we must do is say: **We have nothing to fear**.
There is nothing to fear in moving forward into a better future.
[](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250825.5i8vwY&refcode=em20250825-9616-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9616%2E2526796%2E4hpuwr)
**Thank you** for standing with us,
Alexandria

clean_body: Being an American is about being courageous. This opposition that we’ve seen – all these billionaires that think they can buy our democracy — all they do is tell people who to be afraid of. They’re scared of people coming with nothing but the shirt on their back and trying to start their American Dream. They’re scared of women who own their body and tell them, “No.” They’re scared of gay people, lesbians, and trans people. They’re scared of New York City and “childless cat ladies.” They’re chickensht. To me, being an American is about being courageous. When you ask people for the privilege of taking a role in leading this country, you have to stand up courageously and demand a better world for all people. You have to have the courage to say no to Wall Street and lobbyists and everyone trying to buy our government. You have to have courage to know that an inclusive America is a better America for everybody. While they run around and try to make everybody else as scared as they are, what we must do is say: We have nothing to fear. There is nothing to fear in moving forward into a better future. Thank you for standing with us, Alexandria | ---|--- | | | ---|---|---|--- Email us: mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com

Joni Ernst told us in just seven words exactly what she’ll do with another six years in the Senate.

Your health? Not her problem.

Your life? Not her problem.

Your kids’ future? Not her problem.

I’m one of the 1 in 5 Iowans with a disability. I had 21 surgeries before I was 12 and grew up with the wrong color lunch ticket at school, wearing Goodwill hand-me-downs.

**I’m here because of social safety nets.** They’re lifelines for so many Americans, and I won’t let politicians like Joni Ernst cut them to fund billionaire tax cuts.

Under Joni’s leadership, Iowa’s healthcare system has been pushed to the brink. Denials for medical equipment such as wheelchairs have gone up nearly 1,000%. Our state now has 18,000 disabled citizens on a waiting list for disability services. Rural hospitals are being forced to close. And her vote to kick over 100,000 Iowans off of Medicaid is a punch in the gut to struggling Iowans.

**We’re getting poorer and sicker, and all Joni Ernst can say is, “Well, we all are going to die.”**

**[I’m in this race as a champion for working families, people with disabilities, seniors, veterans, and kids. I’m here to be a voice for those left behind in Joni Ernst’s Iowa. Will you make a founding donation of $7 (or even more!) split with Able Dems to help my team hit our $100,000 launch goal and win Iowa’s U.S. Senate race?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOeD5Hv8jrBs2gR4cymg2NP-r8Sqz5DLMEpmyieK_dpTdBHKbZ1XG_qMQbndNu0fK1IRwvf44NlF3nb-Gr3hnYUAEIyxig8-IGPIgvCAvj5_VYAslRRuJnXREUP0gRywZqkZ0cz3lBtN3QwBldm9jUX6CvMOzWSps59z2wmy0-LLBsMwZXzRcJpYYtqtEa1dOY_ZeW0gTXu5fSEgx2i4XPLF1662bMFlhW_JvUILr9fh3qvtC5p54ZQQWeTI6jjJGVd0A6vy5_fz09gLWhyUHZrzXIMYJ3Ktr5848OEYM1Thi183Yho8ee4CBXcJPStKq46KWZnkCnxSCM8JCDbT4EqPMzuFaJq_oagaRDO1VU2GU1t74Sn2OZ08ZdCbYKUdTbv6xXiO9vGwbrI3fjAgrILhUTPAZoRGB3sV_Pn70_KQ7ir7eSGCDpQUgfMVsTDedCJmjVJ6ep3GvvaQ0LcsTj0g/4jc/uyBUpQApQ6y_fc0D1cL6-w/h3/h001.IUoW7L-XOzB6vujNdAx-Ubc1dAiX1ucpmTjghgXQxxg)**

**TWO:** Local elections like this one will be critical in shaping the agenda for communities across the Peach State and fighting back against the Trump administration at the local level.
**THREE:** That’s why we set a final $10,000 fundraising goal to ensure we have the resources we need to boost voter turnout ahead of tomorrow.
[So Peter, can we count on you to chip in $10 right now to the Democratic Party of Georgia?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113988766/565577649/-818271784?amount=10&source=SBD_DPG_EM_FR_082625_SPECIALELECTION2_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYWQyYjY4YS0wOTgyLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-quTyVOhBYW6sNnqgv-VURPOORbLWmoDGMXkv3GQXFM=)

name: Tobias Read
subject: BREAKING: Oregon Rejects Trump Admin Request to Share Private Voter Data
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250825em-rs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250825em-rs]
Hi, it's Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read.
The Trump Administration is trying to coerce state governments into sharing sensitive voter data.
But as long as I’m your Secretary of State, that will never happen in Oregon.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250825em-rs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250825em-rs]
Let’s be clear about what’s happening here: Donald Trump is leveraging the power of the US Department of Justice to push his false narrative about voter fraud.
Worst of all, he’s pressuring the states to play along — and share your sensitive and personal voter information with the federal government in the process.
I refuse to play a part in Donald Trump’s unrelenting attack on our elections.
But Trump is using the full force of the White House and the federal government to coerce me into compliance, so I need to know that this grassroots team stands with me at this critical moment.
So please, take my 1-question survey and let me know: Do you support Oregon’s fight against the Trump Administration’s attacks on our elections and our democracy? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/auG4lmiVq06681BB7gmzfw2]
ANSWER NOW >> [https://secure.ngpvan.com/auG4lmiVq06681BB7gmzfw2]
Thanks for your support,
Thank you,
Tobias Read
P.S. Don’t forget! The Oregon Political Tax Credit allows you to donate $50 (if you file single) or $100 (if you file joint) to a candidate, PAC, or party, which will be fully credited back as a rebate — so it’s free to donate! For more information, click here. [https://www.oregon.gov/omd/ONG/Documents/SMFS/Personal%20Financial%20Counselor/Political-Tax-Credit.pdf?]
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250825em-rs]
https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR [https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR] https://twitter.com/TobiasRead [https://twitter.com/TobiasRead] https://instagram.com/tobiasreador [https://instagram.com/tobiasreador]

Friends of Tobias Read (5208)
PO Box 42307
Portland, OR 97242
United States

name: Heartland Patriots HQ
subject: Let’s talk about power
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
Our movement is on a mission to take back power from the corrupt politicians and corporate elites tearing up our country. But we need your help to do it, Peter.
#
Let’s talk about power, Peter.
**For decades, corporate-backed politicians have rigged the rules to keep power in the hands of the wealthiest elites in our country.**
They’ve flooded our elections with Big Money. They’ve stacked the deck to drown out our voices. And they’ve worked tirelessly to strip away our freedoms.
_We’re done letting them call the shots._
**Our movement is on a mission to take back power from those corrupt politicians and out-of-touch elites.** We’re determined to put real working people in charge of our country — and that’s why we’re asking for your help today, Peter.
Grassroots candidates — the ones refusing billionaire checks and corporate PAC money — are often fighting the toughest uphill battles in some of the most critical elections. They’re up against smear campaigns, attack ads, and entrenched political machines.
Restoring power to everyday Americans will depend on electing these grassroots-funded candidates, and that is only possible with help from people like YOU.
**[So please, will you chip in $10 or whatever you can to help us support the candidates who are boldly running to take back power for everyday Americans? Every dollar you can give makes a real difference — whether it’s $5, $50, or even more.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94181?refcode=em20250825-9328&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9328%2E614464%2EWmNud3)**

name: Powered by People
subject: The threats are only growing louder
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
#
**Ken Paxton. Greg Abbott. Donald Trump.**
Different names, same corrupt playbook. They lie, abuse power, and rig the system to keep it that way.
Their goal? Turn Texas into a testing ground for far-right extremism—silencing dissent, stripping away rights, and consolidating power.
But here’s the truth: **they’re afraid of people power.** Grassroots organizing works—and they know it. That’s why they’re trying to shut us down.
We’re fighting back by organizing in every corner of Texas—registering new voters, training volunteers, and building year-round infrastructure to win real change. It’s how we’ll beat them at the ballot box.
But these fights aren’t easy. And the threats are only growing louder.
[**If you’re ready to take on Paxton, Abbott, and Trump’s agenda, chip in $3 now to fuel the movement fighting back.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/65845?t=2&akid=240327%2E444211%2EuxT1Oh)
**[DONATE $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/65845?t=3&akid=240327%2E444211%2EuxT1Oh)**
Thank you,
Powered by People

Ill. Gov. JB Pritzker speaks during a press conference in Chicago on Monday, Aug. 25. (Mills Hayes/X)
By Illinois Review

At a dramatic Monday afternoon press conference in downtown Chicago – flanked by nearly every major Democrat in the region – Illinois Gov. J.B. Pritzker delivered a blunt message to President Donald Trump: “Do not come to Chicago.”

The visibly agitated governor, who laced his remarks with personal attacks on the president, vowed to punish anyone who backs Trump’s plan to deploy the National Guard to address the city’s violent crime crisis.

“To any federal official who would come to Chicago and try to incite my people into violence as a pretext for something darker and more dangerous, we are watching – and we are taking names,” Pritzker declared.

* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

**" For their walkout, Texas Democrats get national attention -- and partisan retribution at home," **Texas Tribune's **Kayla Guo** \-- "When Texas House Democrats returned to the Capitol after walking out over the GOP's new congressional map, they were cheered by supporters as bulwarks of democracy -- then promptly bulldozed by Republicans fed up with their protest and intent on further marginalizing the minority party.

"They will be lucky if they get out of this special session without it being a whole lot worse than it would have been had they just stuck around," Rep. **Tom Oliverson** of Cypress and chair of the House Republican Caucus, said last week. "If they had any leverage at the start of the last special session, it's all gone."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/b54cab09-20ad-4546-977c-513666f6fbd9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Democratic lawmakers cast their two-week walkout as a victory for sparking a national movement among blue states for retaliatory redistricting, and for buying time to evaluate a legal challenge to the Republican gerrymander, which aims to net up to five more GOP seats among Texas' 38 congressional districts. The Democrats said it was just the first chapter of their fight, which they intend to continue by working to kill the new lines in court.

But the move also unified typically factious House Republicans, who are now solidly behind a speaker some [once decried](https://substack.com/redirect/58ba6e36-b84e-4ad1-97e2-20789e6b2b98?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as "liberal," and more emboldened to steamroll any Democratic resistance in the remaining weeks of the special session to pass every conservative priority on the agenda -- and then some.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/b54cab09-20ad-4546-977c-513666f6fbd9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"Democrats did a really good job of getting Republicans united," said Arlington GOP Rep. **Tony Tinderholt** , who commended Speaker **Dustin Burrows** ' handling of the walkout after previously opposing his rise to the speakership.

Republicans moved quickly to drive the map through both chambers of the Legislature within a week of the Democrats' return. But they also wasted no time advancing a host of conservative priorities that Democrats vehemently oppose, including bills cracking down on the manufacturing and distribution of abortion pills and requiring transgender people to use the bathroom aligned with their sex assigned at birth in government and school buildings.

Those measures stalled in the House during the regular session. And while Gov. **Greg Abbott** , who controls the special session agenda, had put them on the to-do list from the start, Democrats' protest has only increased the GOP appetite to push them all through as retribution for the walkout.

"Now we're not even going to negotiate," Oliverson said. "We're just going to slam it through, too bad."

Democratic lawmakers condemned the expected retaliation but stood by their walkout, arguing that electoral representation was important enough to fight for, no matter the immediate costs.

"This is the linchpin," House Democratic Caucus Chair Rep.**Gene Wu** of Houston said Friday. "If we don't beat this, if politicians in red or blue states just are free to redistrict after every election cycle to make sure that there's as little dissent as possible -- that's bad for everyone. It's bad for America."

The moment Democratic lawmakers fled the state earlier this month, Republicans [began clamoring](https://substack.com/redirect/81691469-72de-4b5b-9b66-361ed1e2938d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for retribution -- from burying Democrats in fines and stripping them of their committee vice chairmanships to kicking them out of their duly elected offices.

By the time Democrats returned two weeks later, Abbott had [called a new special session](https://substack.com/redirect/4496a714-0c43-43ac-a7b7-7268466a21f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with virtually the [same agenda](https://substack.com/redirect/41480425-9a3e-4f76-958c-989742a9dc16?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that stalled in the first, and Burrows was promising to muscle through every item on the call, "and even some more."

Legislation Democrats oppose was put on a fast track to passage, with the so-called bathroom bill and abortion pill clampdown both set for a hearing last week as soon as the map cleared the House. Burrows said he expected to complete the governor's agenda by Labor Day weekend, almost two weeks before the 30-day session times out.

"There's a definite loss from a negotiating standpoint on many other bills that we did not want to see hit the floor," said Rep. **Terry Canales** of Edinburg, a Democrat who did not participate in the walkout.

The House [adopted the new map](https://substack.com/redirect/bce27f87-9c22-442c-a0c8-93f66c0ae53f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) along party lines on Wednesday, releasing Democratic lawmakers from the [around-the-clock police escort](https://substack.com/redirect/b5304564-fb67-4a85-86d3-97bfe80cfc63?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Burrows had imposed to ensure their attendance.

Just over an hour later, Abbott [expanded the session agenda](https://substack.com/redirect/e91700b3-07f1-4a61-bd1d-b2baf42484bf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to include punishments for lawmakers who "willfully absent themselves" to [block the passage](https://substack.com/redirect/51c3ec43-da4b-4d1e-b6ff-0280a5df7cc7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of legislation.

New penalties are needed, Abbott said in a press release, "to ensure that rogue lawmakers cannot hijack the important business of Texans during a legislative session by fleeing the state."

Republican lawmakers have [filed bills](https://substack.com/redirect/e68a637e-05f8-41f3-a34d-c027a2c1b347?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in both chambers that would declare a member's seat vacant if they are absent for seven consecutive days without permission. The proposal swiftly picked up enough joint and coauthors in the House to pass the chamber, with several key Republicans in leadership signing on. And another measure -- designated [House Bill 18](https://substack.com/redirect/cd78662d-c69b-4aad-9b40-e678ed9bd735?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), signaling it has Burrows' blessing -- would limit political donations to lawmakers who leave the state to stall legislative action, with stiff fines for violators.

The absent Democrats were already subject to daily fines while they were away on their latest quorum break. The final tally amounted to $9,354 for each member, including $500-per-person penalties for every day of the walkout and certain costs incurred in securing their attendance.

"The fines are, for sure, going to be a big issue," Rep. **Jeff Leach** , R-Allen, said. "We're not going to just let those go away."" [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/07e9a801-e840-43a2-b403-de5b721f250f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
