TOP NEWS " For their walkout, Texas Democrats get national attention -- and partisan retribution at home," Texas Tribune's Kayla Guo \-- "When Texas House Democrats returned to the Capitol after walking out over the GOP's new congressional map, they were cheered by supporters as bulwarks of democracy -- then promptly bulldozed by Republicans fed up with their protest and intent on further marginalizing the minority party. "They will be lucky if they get out of this special session without it being a whole lot worse than it would have been had they just stuck around," Rep. Tom Oliverson of Cypress and chair of the House Republican Caucus, said last week. "If they had any leverage at the start of the last special session, it's all gone." Democratic lawmakers cast their two-week walkout as a victory for sparking a national movement among blue states for retaliatory redistricting, and for buying time to evaluate a legal challenge to the Republican gerrymander, which aims to net up to five more GOP seats among Texas' 38 congressional districts. The Democrats said it was just the first chapter of their fight, which they intend to continue by working to kill the new lines in court. But the move also unified typically factious House Republicans, who are now solidly behind a speaker some as "liberal," and more emboldened to steamroll any Democratic resistance in the remaining weeks of the special session to pass every conservative priority on the agenda -- and then some. "Democrats did a really good job of getting Republicans united," said Arlington GOP Rep. Tony Tinderholt , who commended Speaker Dustin Burrows ' handling of the walkout after previously opposing his rise to the speakership. Republicans moved quickly to drive the map through both chambers of the Legislature within a week of the Democrats' return. But they also wasted no time advancing a host of conservative priorities that Democrats vehemently oppose, including bills cracking down on the manufacturing and distribution of abortion pills and requiring transgender people to use the bathroom aligned with their sex assigned at birth in government and school buildings. Those measures stalled in the House during the regular session. And while Gov. Greg Abbott , who controls the special session agenda, had put them on the to-do list from the start, Democrats' protest has only increased the GOP appetite to push them all through as retribution for the walkout. "Now we're not even going to negotiate," Oliverson said. "We're just going to slam it through, too bad." Democratic lawmakers condemned the expected retaliation but stood by their walkout, arguing that electoral representation was important enough to fight for, no matter the immediate costs. "This is the linchpin," House Democratic Caucus Chair Rep.Gene Wu of Houston said Friday. "If we don't beat this, if politicians in red or blue states just are free to redistrict after every election cycle to make sure that there's as little dissent as possible -- that's bad for everyone. It's bad for America." The moment Democratic lawmakers fled the state earlier this month, Republicans for retribution -- from burying Democrats in fines and stripping them of their committee vice chairmanships to kicking them out of their duly elected offices. By the time Democrats returned two weeks later, Abbott had with virtually the that stalled in the first, and Burrows was promising to muscle through every item on the call, "and even some more." Legislation Democrats oppose was put on a fast track to passage, with the so-called bathroom bill and abortion pill clampdown both set for a hearing last week as soon as the map cleared the House. Burrows said he expected to complete the governor's agenda by Labor Day weekend, almost two weeks before the 30-day session times out. "There's a definite loss from a negotiating standpoint on many other bills that we did not want to see hit the floor," said Rep. Terry Canales of Edinburg, a Democrat who did not participate in the walkout. The House along party lines on Wednesday, releasing Democratic lawmakers from the Burrows had imposed to ensure their attendance. Just over an hour later, Abbott to include punishments for lawmakers who "willfully absent themselves" to of legislation. New penalties are needed, Abbott said in a press release, "to ensure that rogue lawmakers cannot hijack the important business of Texans during a legislative session by fleeing the state." Republican lawmakers have in both chambers that would declare a member's seat vacant if they are absent for seven consecutive days without permission. The proposal swiftly picked up enough joint and coauthors in the House to pass the chamber, with several key Republicans in leadership signing on. And another measure -- designated , signaling it has Burrows' blessing -- would limit political donations to lawmakers who leave the state to stall legislative action, with stiff fines for violators. The absent Democrats were already subject to daily fines while they were away on their latest quorum break. The final tally amounted to $9,354 for each member, including $500-per-person penalties for every day of the walkout and certain costs incurred in securing their attendance. "The fines are, for sure, going to be a big issue," Rep. Jeff Leach , R-Allen, said. "We're not going to just let those go away."" body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40069%2E3422442%2EiYoOxi)
Dear Peter,
Republicans in Washington had the opportunity to vote for national independent redistricting commissions when I was in Congress. You know how many Republicans voted for that bill? Zero.
So I thought it was pretty rich when former Speaker Kevin McCarthy went on TV last week to bash California Democrats for asking voters to join us in standing up to Donald Trump and Texas Republicans’ unprecedented mid-decade redistricting.
**[Watch my response>>](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250825_1_ak?refcode=em20250825-40069&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=40069_3422442_iYoOxi&akid=40069%2E3422442%2EiYoOxi)**
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250825_1_ak?refcode=em20250825-40069&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=40069_3422442_iYoOxi&akid=40069%2E3422442%2EiYoOxi)
Kevin wants California to stand by and do nothing while Texas tries to decide the midterm elections before a single vote is cast. But if Kevin and Republicans in Congress had backed that reform, we wouldn't be seeing Texas do what it’s doing right now—and California wouldn’t be forced to respond.
**When I’m traveling the state, I hear from Californians who are glad we’re fighting back.** People I talk to—including some who voted for Trump—think he must follow the Constitution and obey the law.
We need leaders who are willing to stand up to Trump’s attacks. As California’s next governor, I’ll never hesitate to call out Trump’s hypocrisy, fight back against his attacks on our democracy, and protect the rights of all Californians. But I need your help to win.
**[Can you pitch in $5 today to help me win this race—and hold Trump accountable in 2026 and beyond?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250825_1_ak?refcode=em20250825-40069&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=40069_3422442_iYoOxi&akid=40069%2E3422442%2EiYoOxi)**

clean_body: Dear Peter, Republicans in Washington had the opportunity to vote for national independent redistricting commissions when I was in Congress. You know how many Republicans voted for that bill? Zero. So I thought it was pretty rich when former Speaker Kevin McCarthy went on TV last week to bash California Democrats for asking voters to join us in standing up to Donald Trump and Texas Republicans’ unprecedented mid-decade redistricting. Kevin wants California to stand by and do nothing while Texas tries to decide the midterm elections before a single vote is cast. But if Kevin and Republicans in Congress had backed that reform, we wouldn't be seeing Texas do what it’s doing right now—and California wouldn’t be forced to respond. When I’m traveling the state, I hear from Californians who are glad we’re fighting back. People I talk to—including some who voted for Trump—think he must follow the Constitution and obey the law. We need leaders who are willing to stand up to Trump’s attacks. As California’s next governor, I’ll never hesitate to call out Trump’s hypocrisy, fight back against his attacks on our democracy, and protect the rights of all Californians. But I need your help to win. Thank you, Katie Porter Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change.

name: GOTV PAC
subject: “Republicans flip New Jersey?!”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email07132025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email07132025&amount=25]
Peter ,
We wanted to give you an update on what’s happening in New Jersey:
Republicans are seeing a path to winning the governor's mansion -- and it runs right through communities where Democrats lost ground with Black and Hispanic voters in 2024.
A new POLITICO analysis shows GOP nominee Jack Ciattarelli could become the first Republican in over a decade to win if he’s able to replicate Trump’s gains in Black and Hispanic communities and pair them with his suburban base.
That means, our friend, Mikie Sherrill’s race is in major jeopardy, Peter.
If Democrats don’t turn out every voter in urban centers like Camden, Trenton, and Newark, we could wake up on Election Night to the headline: Republicans flip New Jersey.
That’s why GOTV PAC will set up phone and text banks to reach voters who can make a direct difference. We’ll make sure they hear the truth about Ciattarelli’s 100% MAGA agenda: stripping healthcare, raising costs, and backing Trump’s extreme plan to gut government programs.
So will you chip in $10, $25, or whatever you can today to power our phone banks and keep New Jersey in Democratic hands? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email08252025]

name: VoteVets.org
subject: Please, let us explain what Trump’s latest Executive Order really means
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
He aims to use Title 32 as a backdoor to deploy National Guard units from friendly red states as his personal police force in blue states.
#
Peter, we’ll get right to it.
For months we’ve been preparing for Trump to invoke the Insurrection Act so he can deploy the Military against the American people. But today, an Executive Order signed by Trump put us in entirely different territory.
He aims to use Title 32 as a backdoor to deploy National Guard units from MAGA friendly red states as his personal police force in blue states. That means he can deploy the Texas National Guard to California, for example, or the Florida National Guard to New York.
That's what he’s doing in DC as we speak. It’s what he intends to do in the weeks and months to come. Today, it’s to "fight crime" or "crack down" on immigrants. Tomorrow, it could be used to fight fake “voter fraud” in blue states.
This is all public. It’s in a signed Executive Order. He’s not hiding it.
The only thing standing in his way is Congress — if they are willing to cowboy up, exercise their legislative power, and stop Trump’s attacks on our democracy. With GOP majorities in both chambers, we’re not holding our breath.
What that means is we have to be prepared for any outcome next year. We’re going to fight for every vote, the same as we always do, but it’s no exaggeration to say that our work is more important than ever now.
If Republicans won’t stop this dangerous abuse of power, we have to hold them accountable at the ballot box next year. And that work won’t come cheap.
[**So we’re asking you to chip in to the VoteVets Patriot Fund NOW and ensure we have the resources we need to take the fight to Trump and the GOP, from coast-to-coast. Please, donate if you can. It adds up fast.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-august25.hEpWPc&refcode=em250825-1-60&t=1&akid=34560%2E3013755%2EhsJnGt)

name: Gavin Newsom
subject: Bring it
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Election Rigging Response Act, Governor Newsom’s Ballot Measure Committee
body:
The man trying to end our democracy doesn't want an election in California.

Earlier today - from the Oval Office - Donald Trump said the Department of Justice was going to sue to stop the Election Rigging Response Act from going to the ballot.
Yes, the man trying to end our democracy doesn't want an election in California.
Bring it.
Here is the truth: Trump and his allies are going to spend $100 million-plus to beat us. Trump sends ICE agents to threaten and harass people outside our events. He is threatening to end mail-in ballots. On and on it goes.
That is what we are up against. I wish I could tell you this election was going to be easy, but it won't. Trump knows the stakes if Democrats have a fair chance to take back the House, so he is going to use every tool at his disposal to stop us.
But we have what he doesn't have: the support of the people. And if we have the resources to run our campaign, to fund legal fights, and to turn out our voters we're going to win -- and we are going to turn California bluer, nullifying what Texas did.
That means from time to time I have to ask. And given this news, today is one of those days:
[**Can you please make a contribution to my ballot measure committee to help us pass the Election Rigging Response Act? Our average donation is around $30. Many give more. Some give less. The amount doesn't matter; just donate what you can because it all adds up.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/erra-em?refcode=em250825-2&refcode2=2498_4767699_EDBxjw&akid=2498%2E4767699%2EEDBxjw)

name: Adam Schiff (via Democrats.org)
subject: Trump wants me arrested.
body:
---
Derek, it’s Adam Schiff. Donald Trump has repeatedly called for me to be arrested for a variety of imagined offenses, including treason. All because I’ve stood up to him — as the manager of his first impeachment, on the January 6th Committee, and as a U.S. Senator. And like his attacks on universities, law firms, private companies, media organizations — you name it — he wants to try to silence any opposition or independent thought. **President Trump is hoping that threats of political retribution will intimidate those who have been brave enough to oppose him, but his attacks won’t stop me.** I will continue to fight against him and his MAGA cronies to preserve our freedom and democracy. I’m not backing down because I believe in the idea of America. Because I believe that we are capable of standing for freedom — not just at home but abroad. Because I believe that, at our core, we are a nation that takes care of its most vulnerable — not cuts their health care. I’m not alone in fighting back — **the DNC isn’t giving up either.** They’re investing _millions of dollars_ in local organizing and state parties. They’re taking on the Trump administration in court. They are doing the work to elect Democrats up and down the ballot who can act as a check on Trump’s power. **The DNC and I are fighting back because we think this fight is worth it.** We’re fighting back because we know that — even though it won’t be easy — this is a fight that we can win together. To do that, though, we need you to fight back too. **[Derek, if this struggle is important to you too, could you pitch in $3 to the DNC today to help them take on Republicans in 2025 and beyond? Your donation will help organize against Republican candidates, reach voters who need to hear from us, and take on Trump and his administration in court.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250825_4407_fr&amount=3&refcode2=4407_22930333_6ub7Hq&akid=4407%2E22930333%2E6ub7Hq)**
